races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares Beginners Chase

runners.name: Kilbarry Saint
runners.spotlight: Campaigned in Graded company over last 18 months; produced some strong efforts, most notably when second to Only By Night at Exeter last year and to Spindleberry at Clonmel in November; two below-par efforts over fences since but appreciated step back up in trip with good run over hurdles last time at Punchestown; course winner over hurdles has the form to win this.
runners.comment: Some good efforts in Graded races; shaped well over hurdles lto; trip suits, big chance
runners.owner: Mrs Con OKeeffe & Tadhg OSullivan & Anthony OKeeffe

runners.name: Fancy Girl
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point and Cork bumper winner; impressive winner of maiden hurdle in Dec '24 before failing to improve in Graded company twice; step in right direction when second to Topgun Simmy at Down Royal in May; heavy fall at seventh fence on chasing debut at Thurles 21 days ago after ten months off; yard won this last year, acts on all ground and capable of a big run.
runners.comment: Useful point, bumper and maiden hurdle winner; fell heavily on chase debut lto; big player

runners.name: Neon Diamond
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner; ended last season with two below-par efforts but ran encouragingly on chase debut at Naas and backed it up when second to Karamoja at Thurles lto, albeit 12L behind; up in trip and strong stayer.
runners.comment: Good bumper and hurdles form; two decent efforts over fences and step up in trip will suit

runners.name: Gaoth Chuil
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner with some excellent efforts in competitive handicaps; second behind Perceval Legallois at DRF last season off 133; ran to a good level on chase debut at Kilbeggan in April but below form in that sphere on four runs since; needs to improve but very much capable.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler last season; good chase debut in April; below form since but well capable

runners.name: Party Dress
runners.spotlight: Thurles maiden hurdle winner in November 24; well held in handicap off 109 on last hurdles start 343 days ago; won 2m1f Flat handicap in June but lost form since; big ask on chase debut and likely needs better ground.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner; off 157 days since Flat run; chase debut; wants nicer ground

runners.name: Ad Caelum
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles; 0-8 over fences but has been running in better company than this the last twice; well-held in Grade 3 four days ago; drops to a more suitable trip but a bit to find and likely wants better ground.
runners.comment: Useful maiden chaser; drops in class after contesting Graded races last twice; bit to find

races.name: Kilmanahan Beginners Chase

runners.name: Tell Us This
runners.spotlight: Dual handicap hurdle winner, latest at Downpatrick in July off 100; 0-6 over fences but much improved lto when third to Quornofamonday (promoted to second); jumping seems to be coming together with experience; definite chance off unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Best effort yet over fences lto; jumping improving with experiences; chance off same mark

runners.name: Youlita
runners.spotlight: Downpatrick maiden hurdle winner in May 2025; ran just okay in two novice hurdles since; travelled well before finding little when sixth on handicap debut at Down Royal lto; step back in trip looks a good move on chase debut; wouldn't want the ground too soft.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner; found less than promised lto in h'cap; down in trip; chase debut

runners.name: Free Flowing
runners.spotlight: Galway maiden hurdle winner in October 2023 on soft, beating a useful rival in Olympic Man; returned from more than two years off the track when tailed off in conditions hurdle in December; finished tamely again when a distant third at this track lto; needs far more on chase debut in first-time cheekpieces but a player if ability remains.
runners.comment: Useful form, winning maiden in 2023; tailed off twice since long lay-off; bit to prove now

runners.name: Is Charlie Around
runners.spotlight: Back to something like his maiden hurdle form when second to King Kali in handicap hurdle at Limerick in December; third at same venue after; still in the mix when fell two out here over hurdles lto leaving Mount Frisco clear; unseated on only chase start last year; enjoys soft ground.
runners.comment: Good efforts in h'cap hurdles before falling two out when upsides lto; each-way shout

runners.name: Speedy Box
runners.spotlight: Ran a couple of reasonably encouraging races maiden company last season; set to run well behind Brechin Castle before falling two out at Naas lto after a break; open to improvement on chase debut and can be in the mix.
runners.comment: Mild promise in maidens; running well before falling lto; open to progress on chase bow

runners.name: Lagoon Nebula
runners.spotlight: Landed competitive juvenile handicap off 108 at Cheltenham last April on good; good efforts on the Flat since before running below-par at the Curragh on soft; may have needed the run when eighth of nine in mares' handicap hurdle at Cheltenham in December; big player on chase debut but testing ground a concern.
runners.comment: Juvenile handicap winner at Chelt last year; chase debut; soft ground a concern

runners.name: Reliable Prince
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on racecourse debut in January 2025, beaten 12.5l by Coral River; below that form twice since but ran fine in a decent beginners on chase debut at Fairyhouse lto, beaten 36l into sixth; may be one for the longer-term but open to plenty of improvement and an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Good debut when fourth at Gowran last year; ran fine on chase debut lto; open to progress

runners.name: Soldante
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner at Tramore November 2023 before good third in Cheltenham novice hurdle; few below-par efforts after but ran okay when sixth in a strong handicap hurdle at Thurles when last seen; off 431 days since but has the form to go close if ready on chase debut; one of two for a trainer in form.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner; ran ok in strong h'cap hurdle last seen 431 days ago; chance if fit
runners.owner: P McCormack & David P McCormack & Sarah Glynn

runners.name: Miss Dishy
runners.spotlight: Limerick maiden hurdle winner on good in October 2024; has struggled in eight starts since and off 235 days; well held on previous chase effort and hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Lost form since maiden hurdle win in October 24; needs revival but trainer in good form

races.name: Careys Castle Novice Handicap Chase

runners.name: Mount Shenshan
runners.spotlight: Twice placed over fences in October/November; conditions were very testing and she was pulled up (along with many others) in a hurdle race won by Brown Boots here four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Pulled up along with many others in a hurdle race won by Brown Boots here four weeks ago

runners.name: Insouciant Dallier
runners.spotlight: Won a Sligo maiden hurdle last October; not disgraced when sixth in a 24-runner Fairyhouse handicap on New Year's Day; beaten 4l in a beginners' chase at Thurles in January; dropped in class after a stiff task at Navan in his first handicap chase; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Dropped in class after a stiff task at Navan in his first handicap chase, not ruled out

runners.name: Brucejack
runners.spotlight: Still seeking a first chase win after 17 attempts, runner-up three times and has run creditably in the last two editions of the 3m6f Portertstown Chase at Fairyhouse; others seem more likely unless cheekpieces do the trick.
runners.comment: Still seeking a first chase win after 17 attempts, runner-up three times, others preferred

runners.name: Plain Or Battered
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-17 over hurdles; needs to reverse Navan chase form with Brown Boots, but ran well to take third place in a 3m handicap hurdle at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Ran well over hurdles at Leopardstown, could make his presence felt off a lower chase mark
runners.owner: David Norris & Fergal Norris & N Dooly

runners.name: Zolpharine
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 17 starts over hurdles; 0-20 over fences, runner-up on four occasions; hard to be confident on recent evidence.
runners.comment: Only one win from 17 starts over hurdles, 0-20 over fences, hard to be confident

runners.name: Ceroc
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins for the late Edward O'Grady; 0-12 chasing, unplaced in three starts since second over 2m5f at this venue last October.
runners.comment: Unplaced in three starts since second over 2m5f at this venue last October

runners.name: Brown Boots
runners.spotlight: Made instant impact over fences at Navan off 84 (3m, soft to heavy) and ran off the same mark back over hurdles when following up with a 10l at this venue (3m, heavy); not beaten far in third under a fixed penalty at Naas; obvious chance of his marginally low chase mark.
runners.comment: Obvious chance on his hurdles form here and at Naas since a Navan chase win in January

runners.name: Milanaway
runners.spotlight: Punchestown handicap hurdle win in December (3m, heavy) is her sole success in 30 runs; not the most consistent mare but she stays well and has a definite chance on her best form.
runners.comment: Not the most consistent mare but she stays well and has a definite chance on her best form

runners.name: Live To Laugh
runners.spotlight: Fair form over hurdles without winning; won by a big margin at Thurles three weeks ago on his third start over fences; a 10lb rise may prove too much but could challenge for a share of the money.
runners.comment: Won by a big margin at Thurles three weeks ago, a 10lb rise may prove too much
runners.owner: J Whittle/M McEvoy/Good For A Laugh Synd/A Callan

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: Out of form lately; has failed to win in 28 attempts hurdling; less exposed in this sphere, but makes limited appeal.
runners.comment: Out of form lately, has failed to win in 28 attempts hurdling, less exposed in this sphere

runners.name: Nettuno Italia
runners.spotlight: Fair handicap hurdle form; won readily at Punchestown in January, having disappointed several times when well supported; unplaced favourite last time; no chasing experience.
runners.comment: Won readily at Punchestown on penultimate start, record of beaten favouritism is a concern

runners.name: Cordal Boy
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles, no chasing experience; pulled up here four weeks in a handicap hurdle won by Brown Boots; that came after a good effort at Thurles; first time with cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Pulled up here four weeks ago after a good effort at Thurles, first time with cheekpieces

runners.name: Patsys Pride
runners.spotlight: Has been struggling in handicap hurdles; won a point last year; a switch to chasing may help but hard to be confident.
runners.comment: Has been struggling in handicap hurdles, won a point last year, switch to chasing may help

runners.name: Seeitoldya
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner for Ellmarie Holden; showed promise in maiden hurdles and again on handicap debut at Cork last August; was making no impression when unseating rider four out in a 2m4f hurdle on stable debut at Fairyhouse last month.
runners.comment: Was making no impression when unseating rider four out in a 2m4f hurdle on stable debut

races.name: Nire Valley Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: San Hilario
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden over hurdles, runner-up four times, including the last three times; latest when 9.5l behind Hens Tooth at Navan over 2m6f when trip may have stretched him; runner-up in handicap off 118 prior to that so he sets a decent standard back down in trip.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden; runner-up last three times; drop in trip to suit; big chance

runners.name: Boedic
runners.spotlight: Promising racecourse debut when fourth in valuable Fairyhouse bumper last season; underwhelming odds-on favourite at Thurles in December but ran far better when third at Down Royal when mistake two out proved costly; similar level of form when third again at Punchestown lto; drop in trip not sure to suit but a strong contender nonetheless.
runners.comment: Improved efforts over hurdles last twice; down in trip; form good enough to go close

runners.name: Ballinaboola Hanna
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in two bumpers and a similar story over hurdles at Punchestown on penultimate start; in the process of showing more down down in trip before falling two out at Gowran Park last time; still needs a huge step forward to figure.
runners.comment: About to show more before departing two out lto; still needs a big step forward to figure

runners.name: Polepatrick
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite on racecourse bow when runner-up to Marlacoo at Down Royal May 2024; failed to build on that next time but fared better when beaten 7.5l into fifth at Navan 410 days ago in a good bumper won by Copacabana; first-time hood on hurdles debut and horses from this yard typically improve for obstacles.
runners.comment: Promising efforts in bumpers; off 410 days; hurdles debut; interesting in first-time hood

runners.name: Me Wee Nanny Fran
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros 3yo; fifth foal; dam Group-placed 7f/1m winner (inc AW; RPR 102), out of 6f winning half-sister to useful 5f-6f winner Budelli; from top yard and interesting to see what market thinks on debut.
runners.comment: 25,000euros 3yo; dam Group-placed 7f/1m winner (RPR 102); top yard; market can guide

runners.name: Two Can Plan
runners.spotlight: Nothing of note on first two starts over hurdles; showed far more in tongue-tie when beaten 21L into sixth at Leopardstown lto; still below the level required to win this.
runners.comment: Improved markedly on prior runs when beaten 21l into sixth lto; still needs more however

runners.name: Tinto De Verano
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when fourth at Wexford and Leopardstown in bumpers; below that form at Thurles in softer ground; needs more on hurdles debut
runners.comment: Below form lto at Thurles in softer ground; needs more on hurdles debut

runners.name: Nameyourgame
runners.spotlight: Finished third of four in Oldtown point-to-point in February, beaten 4l; pulled up 18 days ago at Castlelands; rules debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Pulled up 18 days ago at Castlelands; rules debut; best watched.

runners.name: Jet Renegade
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in three bumpers, particularly when third to Mullaghgloss and Zeus Power on racecourse debut; two decent runs in Flat maidens the last twice, latest 41 days ago when beaten 7l into sixth over 1m4f at Dundalk; could hit the frame on hurdles debut but soft ground a concern.
runners.comment: Showed promise in three bumpers and two AW runs; soft ground a concern on hurdles debut

runners.name: Knowing Me
runners.spotlight: Well held in three starts and didn't fare any better after long absence when down the field at Naas last time; more of a long-term prospect.
runners.comment: Well held in four starts; more of a long-term prospect

runners.name: Karlynn
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros 2yo; half-brother to winners Authentic Eye (German 1m2f/10.5f) and Olkar (French 2m2f-2m3f hurdle/chase); beaten a long way on hurdles debut at Gowran Park last time; open to improvement but hard to envisage him figuring.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on hurdles debut at Gowran Park lto; hard to see him figuring

runners.name: Home By The Sea
runners.spotlight: Last of five in Fairyhouse bumper and down the field on hurdles debut at same track lto; hasn't shown enough to suggest he can be competitive here.
runners.comment: Well held in bumper and maiden hurdle; hard to see him making his mark here

runners.name: Figero
runners.spotlight: Green when well held in two bumpers during the winter; not an obvious contender on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Green when well held in two bumpers during the winter; big ask on hurdles debut

runners.name: Hey Now
runners.spotlight: Shaped nicely on racecourse debut when fourth in Naas bumper 101 days ago, racing plenty keenly and just fading late on having made a significant move to hit the front turning for home; form has worked out okay and he could easily progress now tackling hurdles; wants further on pedigree but capable of going close.
runners.comment: Shaped with plenty of promise when fourth in Naas bumper; in the mix on hurdles debut

runners.name: Checkpoint Georgie
runners.spotlight: 42,000eyros 3yo; half-brother to winners Hollygrove Cha Cha (bumper and useful 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase) and Sheila Nash (bumper/2m7f hurdle); tailed off on debut at Gowran Park 19 days ago; should improve but unlikely to make presence felt.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut at Gowran Park 19 days ago; should improve but huge ask

runners.name: Colesberg
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Celestial Park (2m4f hurdle) and Global Fortune (point); dam unraced sister to useful 2m5f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Warden Hill; tailed off on hurdles debut at Gowran Park 19 days ago; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Tailed off on hurdles debut at Gowran Park 19 days ago; hard to fancy.

runners.name: Kome Dans Un Reve
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle starts at Tramore and Kilbeggan, well beaten in both; off 336 days; best watched on return.
runners.comment: Two hurdle starts at Tramore and Kilbeggan, well beaten both; off 336 days; best watched

runners.name: Seabloom
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros 3yo; fourth foal; half-brother to point winner Matterofexpression; dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Pride Of The Parish (bumper/2m4f hurdle) and Emily Gray (2m-2m5f hurdle/chase); 15th of 18 in valuable Punchestown bumper 331 days ago; improvement likely; could figure for top yard on stable debut if getting a run.
runners.comment: Reserve; 55,000euros 3yo; down the field in valuable bumper a year ago; top yard

runners.name: Shabra Diya
runners.spotlight: 0-8 in bumpers, couple of decent efforts in that sphere at Thurles and Ballinrobe on good ground; tailed off at Newbury last time when a big price on hurdles debut; needs plenty more and wants a decent surface.
runners.comment: Couple of decent efforts in bumpers; struggled in soft ground, tailed off the last twice

races.name: Adare Manor Opportunity Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Sound Judgement
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer who was well-backed when third behind Lilannbee in Punchestown bumper in December; pulled up on hurdles debut at Fairyhouse after but back on track when 11l second to Galileo Dame at Navan 26 days ago; big player if building on that.
runners.comment: Bounced back after being PU with good second at Navan lto; big player if building on that

runners.name: Tangara Bay
runners.spotlight: Good second behind prolific bumper performer Seo Linn on debut at Ballinrobe in August; stayed on well to finish fourth on hurdles debut at Roscommon; similar form back down in trip when 14l fifth behind El Cairos 56 days ago; step up in trip will suit and definite claims.
runners.comment: Ran to similar level to hurdles debut behind El Cairos lto; step up in trip sure to suit

runners.name: Miss Georgina
runners.spotlight: 0-4 over hurdles but has run in some strong maidens won by Daboya and Bambino Fever; probably found extended 2m4f a stretch last time at Leopardstown; each-way chance back down in trip.
runners.comment: Has shown ability in four starts over hurdles; drop in trip should suit; can be in the mix

runners.name: Jury Belle
runners.spotlight: Encouraging second in bumper on debut at Worcester in June before disappointing favourite at Stratford; two okay runs over hurdles after and shaped well at Thurles 161 days ago, beaten 7l into fourth by Run for Mahler; chance on that form but might need better ground.
runners.comment: Worcester bumper winner; good fourth at Thurles 161 days ago; might need sounder surface

runners.name: Early Bird
runners.spotlight: Useful form in bumpers, winning at Tramore on third attempt in May 2025 on good; shaped well enough in a good mares maiden hurdle at Naas in December, beaten 17.5l seventh by Oldschool Outlaw; ran about as well as could be expected again behind that rival in Grade 3 company lto; calmer waters now and big chance back down in trip.
runners.comment: Useful bumper form; two respectable runs over hurdles, well beaten in G3 lto; big chance

runners.name: Isdistheone
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in six bumper runs; best effort when third at thos track in November; well beat on hurdles debut at Fairyhouse and ran to a similar level lto at Cork; needs more.
runners.comment: Best run came here in a bumper in October; needs more based on last two hurdle runs

runners.name: Chautuaqua
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 15 starts came at this track in a bumper on debut in March 2022 on heavy; in the frame in maiden company a couple of times since; good second at Gowran Park lto; up in trip; this a stronger contest however so needs more.
runners.comment: Sole win in 15 starts came here in a bumper; good second lto; this a stronger race

runners.name: Windy Grey
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to point winner Yeoman Warder; showed very little when tailed off in weak mares maiden at Gowran Park 19 days ago; hard to see her making an impact.
runners.comment: Tailed off, showed little on hurdles debut lto; hard to make a case for

runners.name: Mysilverriverfeale
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when finishing midfield in two bumpers last season; returned from a break when well beaten in sixth on hurdles debut at Thurles 21 days ago; will improve for that and claims of figuring in first-time hood.
runners.comment: Showed ability in two bumpers last season; should come on for hurdles debut; could figure

runners.name: Mind The Steps
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in two point-to-points; too free but ran fine for a long way before folding quickly at Thurles 21 days ago; needs to settle better and best to look elsewhere
runners.comment: Too free on hurdles debut when folding markedly at Thurles; best to look elsewhere

runners.name: Lady Aquarelle
runners.spotlight: Fell in two point-to-points and well held sixth at Dromahane; nothing to suggest she can get off the mark here after hurdle runs at Punchestown and Clonmel; down in trip.
runners.comment: Down the field in two hurdle runs this year; down in trip; hard to fancy

runners.name: Sarahs Express
runners.spotlight: Showed bits and pieces of ability in four runs prior pulling up over 3m at Punchestown last time; drop back in trip will suit but still needs plenty more.
runners.comment: Found 3m too far when pulling up lto; showed hints of promise prior; needs plenty more

races.name: Glenary Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Finnicky Filly
runners.spotlight: Twice pulled up at this venue after winning here in December; finished to some effect from a long way off the pace at Naas early this month; not far off sixth-placed Lissen To The Lady who was entitled to need the race.
runners.comment: Twice pulled up at this venue after winning here in December, far from reliable

runners.name: Courtbrack Boy
runners.spotlight: No show in maidens; solid fourth on handicap debut at Listowel last September (2m, good to yielding); detached at Thurles next start; runner-up of 14 here in January, 23l behind an impressive winner; went one place better four weeks ago with a 7l success; 7lb rise is not particularly harsh.
runners.comment: Course winner over a shorter trip four weeks ago, 7lb rise is not particularly harsh
runners.owner: Michael P Murray & Timothy R Murray

runners.name: La Tene
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in 2023; has made no impact in three runs since returning from a long absence; 100-1 when pulled-up at Limerick a fortnight ago; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: No impact in three runs since returning from a long absence, seems safe to rule out
runners.owner: John Patrick Ryan & Miss Gillian Ryan

runners.name: Bite That
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner but consistent in the main and deserved his Wexford chase victory a year ago; has failed to take advantage of lower hurdles mark in three outings since then; tailed off here four weeks ago in the race won by Courtbrack Boy.
runners.comment: Has failed to take advantage of his lower hurdles mark in three outings since a chase win

runners.name: Bella Union
runners.spotlight: Never in the first three in 15 starts over hurdles; single-figure odds in four of his five races this season, but never closer than fifth; hard to be confident.
runners.comment: Single-figure odds in four of his five races this season but never closer than fifth

runners.name: Larkins Lane
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on handicap debut at Limerick last May; in rear at Wexford in July; stayed on into a remote fourth over 2m4f at Fairyhouse in January on first start for Philip Rothwell; may improve.
runners.comment: Remote fourth over 2m4f at Fairyhouse on first start for Philip Rothwell, may improve

runners.name: Falcon Park
runners.spotlight: Had been struggling to find his best form since joining this yard before an encouraging second behind a well-handicapped winner at this venue last month; failed to build on that at Cork; reserve.
runners.comment: Reserve, returned to form with a second at this venue, failed to build on that at Cork

runners.name: Lissen To The Lady
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner in Britain and gained her first Irish win in game fashion over 2m4f at this venue in January 2024 on her third and final start for Marie Harding; at the veteran stage now but made a promising reappearance under Joanna Walton (aboard for all seven wins) over 2m at Naas recently; each-way prospects.
runners.comment: All seven wins for this rider, each-way prospects now following an encouraging comeback

runners.name: Anodandawink
runners.spotlight: Out of his depth in maidens, always at big odds; finished second in a point when with Derek O'Connor; first handicap, worth a check in the market.
runners.comment: Second in a point, no show in maidens, first handicap, worth a check in the market

runners.name: Ballinaboola Gold
runners.spotlight: Took a small step in the right direction with a midfield run at Punchestown; 25-1 when in rear on handicap debut at Naas; held by Lissen To The Lady and Finnicky Filly.
runners.comment: In rear on handicap debut at Naas, held by Lissen To The Lady and Finnicky Filly

runners.name: Uncle Gerhard
runners.spotlight: Veteran's sole win came at Gowran in January 2022 for Conor O'Dwyer; signs of life when sixth of 20 at Down Royal in December; attracted market support at Thurles last month, never threatened; more like it when 8l fourth of 11 at Gowran last time.
runners.comment: Four years since his only win, bits and pieces of form this term, fair fourth on latest

runners.name: Lucky Crystal
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in four races; transformation needed with tongue-tie fitted on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off in four races, transformation needed with tongue-tie fitted on handicap debut

runners.name: Embrace The Day
runners.spotlight: Looked promising when winning a maiden point at Loughrea last May for Jonathan Fogarty; three inconclusive maiden hurdle runs; ignored in the market on handicap debut, completely tailed off.
runners.comment: Point winner, inconclusive maiden form, ignored in the market on handicap debut, no show

runners.name: Loi Des Jeux
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts in maiden hurdles at 2m-2m5f on varying ground; needs to step up from handicap debut at Punchestown in January.
runners.comment: Modest form, needs to step up from handicap debut at Punchestown in January

runners.name: Ixigreczed
runners.spotlight: Sole success off at Kilbeggan (2m3f, gd-yld) in June 2024; never better than sixth in eight (hurdles and chases) in 2025; blinkered for the first time now.
runners.comment: Kilbeggan winner in 2024, weak form as hurdler/chaer in 2025, blinkered first time now

runners.name: Silent Guardian
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden; mild promise in three maiden hurdles; moderate seventh on handicap debut at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Mild promise in three maiden hurdles; moderate seventh on handicap debut at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Lake Okeechobee
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Faisryhouse on the most recent of three maiden hurdle starts; her chance is not obvious
runners.comment: Tailed off on the most recent of three maiden hurdle starts, her chance is not obvious

races.name: Glenary Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Spellacys Cross
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins last October at this venue, including over this trip; failed to land a blow from 4lb out of handicap in 3m4f Cork National in November; looks a tempting proposition off his 18lb lower hurdles mark, given his record at the venue.
runners.comment: Twice a course winner over fences, a tempting proposition off his 18lb lower hurdles mark
runners.owner: James M Barcoe & J F ONeill

runners.name: Marlpark
runners.spotlight: Weak form since a course maiden win in October 2024; 200-1 chance in a conditions race here in January; big drop in class last time at Gowran last time, faded badly in the straight.
runners.comment: Weak form since a course maiden win in October 2024, not an obvious candidate

runners.name: Ballyredinkingston
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; in rear in two handicaps; no appeal despite rider's valuable claim
runners.comment: Weak maiden form, in rear in two handicaps, no appeal despite rider's valuable claim

runners.name: Qaasid
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on the Flat in Britain; 0-10 hurdling for this yard, best effort yet when runner-up in a claimer here last month; down the field back in handicap company at Cork last week; reserve.
runners.comment: Reserve, second in a claimer at this venue last month, handicap form is weak
runners.owner: Four Fools & A Fast Horse Syndicate

runners.name: Ode To Joy
runners.spotlight: No sign of worthwhile form in bumpers/maiden hurdles; showed improvement in first-time hood when fourth at Naas on handicap debut; could feature.
runners.comment: Showed improvement in first-time hood when fourth at Naas on handicap debut, could feature

runners.name: Ossifer Hops
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in 2023 but has failed to recapture form since joining this yard; 50-1 when pulled up in a 3m handicap here four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won a maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in 2023, has failed to recapture form for this yard

runners.name: The Cobblers Wife
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; 100-1 shot when hampered and unseated rider at the seventh flight at Punchestown on her handicap debut; 100-1 again when tailed off at Naas early this month.
runners.comment: Weak maiden form, no sign of winning potential in two handicap starts, 100-1 both times

runners.name: Opposite Ends
runners.spotlight: Not a bad effort on handicap debut after long absence when fifth at Thurles in November; unseated, brought down next two starts; tailed off at Limerick before a much-improved display at this venue four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Mixed form, tailed off at Limerick before a much-improved display at this venue last time

runners.name: Added Bonus
runners.spotlight: Veteran has won twice over fences at Thurles; however, he has failed to make the frame in 15 consecutive starts since a Naas second in December 2024.
runners.comment: Has failed to make the frame in 15 consecutive starts since a Naas second in December 2024

runners.name: Market Ready
runners.spotlight: Fair fourth in 2m maiden hurdle here on debut a year ago; out of his depth in Punchestown festival bumper following month; managed a creditable third placing behind I'm Slippy (now rated 128) in Cork maiden in October; should be sharper after a run in a Gowran maiden earlier in the month; definite chance in first handicap.
runners.comment: Fair chance based on Cork third in October, should be sharper now after a run at Gowran

runners.name: Dark Lieutenant
runners.spotlight: Two respectable bumper runs; two most recent of four hurdle outings offer no encouragement.
runners.comment: Two respectable bumper runs, two most recent of four hurdle outings offer no encouragement

runners.name: Strand Street Lass
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in two point-to-points; in rear in two maiden hurdles before a slightly more promising run in mid-division at Thurles Josh Williamson's claim is the main positive.
runners.comment: Mid-division at Thurles on third maiden run, Josh Williamson's claim is the main positive

runners.name: Potters Pal
runners.spotlight: Modest form in point-to-points; well held in three maiden hurdles; never a factor on Punchestown handicap debut; needs to step up considerably after fifth of nine over a shorter trip here four weeks ago; hood added to tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Needs to step up considerably after fifth of nine over a shorter trip here four weeks ago

runners.name: Boston Breeze
runners.spotlight: Took a step in the right direction when a remote fourth at Thurles on third maiden outing; of some interest.
runners.comment: Step in the right direction when a remote fourth on third maiden outing, of some interest

runners.name: Humble Brag
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three maiden hurdles; stayed on into a remote fourth over C&D in his first handicap; may improve.
runners.comment: Stayed on into a remote fourth over C&D in his first handicap, may improve

runners.name: Brave Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Won a point-to-point in 2023 before losing form; two moderate maiden hurdle runs after a long absence.
runners.comment: Won a point-to-point in 2023, two moderate maiden hurdle runs after a long absence

runners.name: Live My Dream
runners.spotlight: Fair run at Limerick in November; pulled up at Punchestown last month; hard to fancy despite the fact that Darragh O'Keeffe sticks with him.
runners.comment: Pulled up on latest, hard to fancy despite the fact that Darragh O'Keeffe sticks with him

races.name: Next Race Meeting April 2nd (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Detroit Maverick
runners.spotlight: Point winner for Colin Bowe; second in two bumper attempts at Fairyhouse; beaten 3/4l by The Wager who has since finished seventh of 21 (second-best for Willie Mullins) in the Champion Bumper at Cheltenham; beaten 5l by the Mullins-trained Look Me a month ago, form franked at Limerick by third-placed Bois d'Angos.
runners.comment: Point winner, second to a Mullins-trained favourite in two bumper runs, form is very solid

runners.name: Brickfield Belle
runners.spotlight: Well-related Blue Bresil mare; made a satisfactory debut when fifth as stable's second-string in a 21-runner maiden hurdle at Naas; finished 30l behind the third horse, but likely to improve.
runners.comment: Remote but satisfactory fifth on debut in a maiden hurdle at Naas, likely to improve

runners.name: Scarecrow
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to the trainer's talented Getabird (seven wins, including two bumpers and two Grade 2 novice hurdle), and recent dual handicap hurdle winner Catchabird (2m hurdle); fitted with a tongue-tie for debut; almost sure to be in the mix.
runners.comment: Half-brother to the talented Getabird, tongue-tie for debut, almost sure to be in the mix

runners.name: Ballydiamond
runners.spotlight: First foal; unraced dam out of unraced sister to two winners; career has got off to an unpromising start; unseated at the first in two point-to-point appearances; not a betting proposition.
runners.comment: Unseated at the first in two point-to-point appearances, not a betting proposition

runners.name: Ballydine Boy
runners.spotlight: E20,000 3yo; second foal; dam, by Presenting, a hurdle/chase winner who stayed well, sister to three winners; dead-heated at Belclare early this month on second point start; may find this trip a bit sharp.
runners.comment: Has a staying pedigree, dead-heated in a recent point, may find this trip a bit sharp

runners.name: Go On Ta
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam pulled up in bumper, half-sister to a point winner Emma Blue, from family of the smart Norse Dancer; makes belated debut at seven for yard with 0-25 bumper record over the past five seasons.
runners.comment: Makes belated debut at seven for yard with 0-25 bumper record over the  last five seasons

runners.name: Tadhgs Rock
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in both point starts in 2024; solid effort in a Thurles bumper just over a year ago; an absence to overcome but likely capable of a decent showing.
runners.comment: Solid effort in a Thurles bumper just over a year ago, could go well if fitness holds up

runners.name: Facile Ocean
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Fascile Mode, a bumper/hurdler/chase winner for Tom Mullins; favourite for that yard on Naas debut last season, too keen in a slowly-run race when third of four; again too keen at Ballinrobe last April on first start for Ciaran Murphy; best watched.
runners.comment: Raced too keenly in two outings last season, favourite on debut, best watched

runners.name: Our Sara
runners.spotlight: Never threatened when 28l seventh of ten at Thurles in November; unlikely to feature.
runners.comment: Never threatened when 28l seventh of ten at Thurles in November unlikely to feature
runners.owner: Mrs Anne Gleeson/Miss Heather Heffernan/Gerard P ONeill

races.name: Feldon Dunsmore Proudly Employee-Owned Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Big Ticket
runners.spotlight: Point winner who was runner-up on first two hurdle starts (including on good ground) and won a maiden at Lingfield (2m3f, heavy) last month; similar form when third of five in handicap at Doncaster (3m, soft) since; carries a 7lb penalty but still stands out as the one to beat.
runners.comment: Carries penalty for maiden hurdle win at Lingfield but still stands out as the one to beat

runners.name: La Vish
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability when remote third to two useful sorts on hurdle debut at Ludlow (2m5f, good to soft) in January but beaten 52l when third of four here (2m3f, soft) since and has plenty to find today.
runners.comment: Has shown ability but remote third on both hurdle starts and has plenty to find today

runners.name: Bobbarelli
runners.spotlight: 9yo who was 2-12 in points (the wins coming over 2m4f on good/good to firm); down the field on hurdle debut at Doncaster (2m3f, soft) last month but that performance wasn't without promise and today's better ground could help; he may be the main threat to stablemate Big Ticket.
runners.comment: 2-12 in points; on better ground for second hurdle start; may be main threat to Big Ticket

races.name: JCB Mini-Excavator Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Getaway Sam
runners.spotlight: 0-8; achieved closest finish yet when sixth of 11 at Hereford (2m3f, good) recently, beaten just over 7l on that third handicap start; wears first-time cheekpieces and has major claims in a race very much lacking depth.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when sixth at Hereford; has major claims in a race very much lacking depth

runners.name: Hitthekettle
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when fourth over hurdles at Stratford last July and he's now 9lb lower; this 10yo is now 0-12 under rules having struggled on his three starts since but those were over fences; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 10yo; 0-12 under rules but has fallen down the weights & the return to hurdles could help

runners.name: El Arco
runners.spotlight: No improvement for the switch to handicaps and he's been tailed off at 80-1 on both attempts in this sphere (2m4f/2m3f, good to soft/soft).
runners.comment: No improvement for switch to h'caps and tailed off at 80-1 on both attempts in this sphere

runners.name: Miss Jaffa Cake
runners.spotlight: Signs of promise prior to unseating in Wincanton novice (2m5f, heavy) in January but tailed off/pulled up on both runs since, most recently when 40-1 for handicap debut at Stratford (2m6f, soft).
runners.comment: Hasn't been devoid of promise but she was pulled up at 40-1 on her recent handicap debut

runners.name: Kampari
runners.spotlight: He's raced eight times and has never come close to posing a serious threat; well beaten at 33-1 on recent handicap debut at Sedgefield (2m4f, good).
runners.comment: Has never come close to posing a serious threat; well beaten at 33-1 on recent h'cap debut

runners.name: Silver Gale
runners.spotlight: Third in a point-to-point bumper last April; tailed off at massive odds on her three hurdle starts (2m/2m3f) but now goes handicapping at a longer trip off a lowly mark and there's stamina in her pedigree; interesting.
runners.comment: Now goes handicapping at a longer trip off lowly mark and there's stamina in her pedigree

runners.name: Ffree Pedro
runners.spotlight: Won three points in May/June 2024 but then absent until this winter, and he's been pulled up over fences (50-1) then hurdles (33-1) on his two outings this season; a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: Three point wins in May/June 2024 but pulled up on both runs this season, after absence

races.name: Best Odds Guaranteed At Betano Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: El Rojo Grande
runners.spotlight: Kicked off 2026 with a hat-trick (2m1f/2m, soft/heavy) and he's gone close on his last two starts (2m/2m3f, heavy/soft); has shown promise on good ground in the past and he could be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Three wins and two near-misses so far in 2026; could be bang there once more
runners.owner: Genghis Racing & Mike Harris Racing Club

runners.name: Claim Du Brizais
runners.spotlight: Bumper and novice hurdle winner who is in top hands and has dropped down the weights; could rebound at some stage but she needs to produce more than she's managed on her first three chase starts.
runners.comment: In top hands but needs to produce more than she's managed on her first three chase starts
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate - Claim Du

runners.name: Kingoftheswingerz
runners.spotlight: Won at 22-1 on chase debut at Worcester (2m4f, good) in September on first run since May; disappointing at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good to soft) in October but often runs well after a break and he could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Won on chase debut in September; disappointing in October last time but can go well fresh

runners.name: Sawpit Samantha
runners.spotlight: 0-14 over hurdles and soundly beaten last of three on last month's chase debut at Leicester; others are preferred.
runners.comment: 0-14 over hurdles & soundly beaten last of three on last month's chase debut at Leicester

runners.name: Full Force Gale
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning a bumper on debut in October 2024 and has struggled to get competitive on his three hurdle starts; sent straight over fences in first-time headgear for this handicap debut and a market check is advised, but others arrive with more compelling credentials.
runners.comment: Market check advised on chase/handicap debut but others have more compelling credentials

runners.name: Harthill
runners.spotlight: Just the one good run over hurdles this season, having missed all of 2024 and most of 2025; however, she was nominated as a future chaser by her trainer back in 2023 and she's not discounted in first-time cheekpieces on this first outing over fences.
runners.comment: Patchy form over hurdles this season but could be the type to improve for fences

runners.name: Drumheller
runners.spotlight: Mixed form over hurdles this season but runner-up at Plumpton (3m1f, good) two starts ago and stable has much better strike-rate with chasers than hurdlers nowadays; not ruled out on first run over fences.
runners.comment: Mixed form over hurdles this season but could improve for fences and he's not ruled out

runners.name: Kap De Triomphe
runners.spotlight: Irish bumper winner who got off the mark over hurdles at the ninth attempt in a handicap at Plumpton (2m4f, good to soft) last Monday; did it easily and has the same mark for today's chase debut (due to be 7lb higher in future); leading claims if transferring that latest form to fences.
runners.comment: Easy win off this mark over hurdles at Plumpton last Monday; now goes chasing

runners.name: Knightsbridge
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who could be suited by the drop back in trip on this chase debut; he's not written off today but needs to take a step forward from his hurdle form if he's to come out on top.
runners.comment: The drop back in trip could help on chase debut but he has to improve upon his hurdle form

races.name: Betano Boosted Odds Every UK Race Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ip Up
runners.spotlight: Her losing sequence goes back 15 races to January 2024 but she's been running consistently well this season; versatile ground-wise and can be thereabouts once more.
runners.comment: Running consistently well in defeat this season and can be thereabouts once more

runners.name: Gower Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won Uttoxeter maiden (2m4f, heavy) in November and has also shown promise in some handicaps subsequently; however, well beaten at Hereford (2m, good) recently and there is a question mark about the forecast good ground.
runners.comment: Has shown promise in some handicaps but well beaten at Hereford (2m, good) recently

runners.name: Lady Magic
runners.spotlight: Two wins in bumpers in France and off the mark at the second attempt over hurdles at Kempton (2m, good to soft) last month; failed to build on that when fifth of 13 in Wincanton novice (1m7f, good to soft) since but she nevertheless brings some potential to this handicap debut; could be involved.
runners.comment: Kempton maiden winner who brings some potential to this handicap debut; could be involved

runners.name: Ajs Diamond
runners.spotlight: Placed in two bumpers (including on good ground) and has also shown promise over hurdles, finishing third of 18 in Wincanton maiden (1m7f, good to soft) last time; it's possible she will improve for a longer trip but this unexposed 6yo is still entitled to respect on today's handicap debut.
runners.comment: Third of 18 in Wincanton maiden last month and entitled to respect on handicap debut

runners.name: Mistral Milly
runners.spotlight: She's on a competitive mark on what she showed in 2024 for Emma Lavelle and she was returning from a long absence when tailed off at 80-1 at Haydock three weeks ago; however, it's hard to be confident about her claims.
runners.comment: Competitive mark on 2024 form but tailed off at 80-1 recently following long absence
runners.owner: John Murray & Exor Of Late Niall Farrell

runners.name: Pure Seduction
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at 14-1 on handicap debut at Doncaster (2m3f, soft) last month when back from a long absence, and she has to prove how much ability she retains; however, she may have needed that latest run and it's too soon to write off this very lightly raced, good-ground novice hurdle winner.
runners.comment: Tailed off last month but after a long absence; it's too soon to write her off

races.name: JCB Handicap Chase (Challenger Staying Chase Series Final) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Get Up Mush
runners.spotlight: He's returned from an absence this season and, after three defeats on soft ground, better could have been expected on good to soft at Wincanton recently; however, he's in top hands and well handicapped on his three 2024 wins on good/good to soft (including here), and may bounce back at some stage.
runners.comment: Plenty to prove on this season's evidence but in top hands & may bounce back at some stage

runners.name: The Famous Five
runners.spotlight: Having dropped back down to his last winning mark he returned to form when third in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Wincanton (3m1f, good to soft) three weeks ago; he's a contender if backing up that effort.
runners.comment: Returned to form when third in cheekpieces at Wincanton; a contender if backing that up

runners.name: Pilsdon Pen
runners.spotlight: Wincanton specialist who kept on for second there (2m4f, good to soft) three weeks ago in first-time cheekpieces (retained); he's run well here in the past but needs to prove his stamina, having faded over 3m1f at Wincanton in December.
runners.comment: Ran well over 2m4f at favourite course Wincanton three weeks ago; unproven at today's trip

runners.name: Fever Dream
runners.spotlight: Following a break he reverted to fences at Kempton (3m) 12 days ago and returned to form with a 3l second on his favoured good ground; that performance reads well and he's high on the list today.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Kempton 12 days ago; that performance reads well; high on the list today
runners.owner: Bedford & Noel & The Cant Say No

runners.name: New Order
runners.spotlight: Weakened when well-beaten third at Doncaster (3m2f, soft) last month and needs to bounce back; however, he was running very well in defeat otherwise this winter (3m/3m1f, good-soft) and this C&D winner will be in the mix if back on song this afternoon.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Doncaster last time but running very well in defeat previously

runners.name: Imperial Alex
runners.spotlight: In top form over 3m on good ground last May/June, finishing second at Uttoxeter then first at Perth; soundly beaten on both runs since but those were on heavy/soft going and perhaps today's return to a better surface will trigger a revival.
runners.comment: In top form on good going last May/June; the return to better ground may trigger a revival

runners.name: Lord Accord
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at the Cheltenham Festival a fortnight ago when back from a break; this 11yo is back in much calmer waters today and he's a player if rediscovering the good front-running form he was showing on good/good to soft ground in the autumn when these blinkers first went on.
runners.comment: In good form in the autumn and when pulled up last time it was at the Cheltenham Festival

runners.name: Yes Day
runners.spotlight: He hasn't been at his best this season but he didn't run badly in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Sandown (3m, soft) recently and could build on that now back on better ground, with last season's two wins coming on good/good to firm; now 6lb below last winning mark; interesting.
runners.comment: Below best this season but reduced mark can help, and possibly the better ground too

races.name: JCB Tele-Handler "Junior" National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ivor Bear
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth of 16 on debut in Listed bumper at Cheltenham (2m1f, good) in January before 9l second to well-bred newcomer at Doncaster (2m, soft); holds leading form claims today.
runners.comment: Promise on both starts and he holds leading form claims

runners.name: Nightflyer
runners.spotlight: Made winning start at Catterick (1m7f, soft) in January when with Adrian Keatley; weak in the market and well beaten on stable debut at Haydock (1m7f, good to soft) three weeks ago but was carried badly right by Order Driven at the first bend; not written off today.
runners.comment: Made winning start in January; well beaten on stable debut since but had an excuse

runners.name: Saint Argente
runners.spotlight: 60,000euros 3yo; third foal; brother to French 1m7f-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Bellodam; dam French 2m2f hurdle winner, sister to two winners notably top-class Djakadam; wears a hood on debut, which strikes a cautionary note, but trainer does well in bumpers here and he's one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Wears a hood on debut but yard does well in bumpers here and he's one to be interested in

runners.name: Sevenkingsmustdie
runners.spotlight: 425,000gns yearling in 2023, unraced on the Flat; second foal; brother to 1m1f winner Loveable Rogue (RPR 72); dam half-sister to Group 3 winners Burnt Sugar (6f 2yo) and Brown Sugar (5f/6f 2yo), out of useful 5f/6f winner; wears a hood on debut; in-form trainer is doing well in bumpers and he's an intriguing newcomer, all the more so if attracting market support.
runners.comment: 425,000gns yearling in 2023, unraced on Flat; the betting could be informative on debut

runners.name: Affinitive
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced half-sister to three useful winners notably Stellar Notion (bumper and 2m-2m7f hurdle/chase); in-form trainer can ready one first time and he could have a part to play on debut.
runners.comment: Unraced dam from useful family; in-form trainer can ready one first time

runners.name: Krackerjack
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unplaced bumpers/hurdles, half-sister to two fairly useful winners, out of 2m chase winner; watching brief advised on debut.
runners.comment: Dam unplaced, half-sister to two fairly useful winners; watching brief advised on debut

runners.name: The Honorable Norm
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam bumper/2m5f chase winner (RPR 107), half-sister to two winners, out of unraced mare from talented family; trainer's horses are running well but he's 0-41 in bumpers in the last five seasons.
runners.comment: First foal; dam bumper/2m5f chase winner (RPR 107); may be one for further down the line

runners.name: Aribidia
runners.spotlight: Backed into 17-2 when fifth of 14 on last month's debut at Kempton (2m, good to soft); he was soundly beaten and improvement is necessary but he has an attractive Flat pedigree and may be able to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Has an attractive Flat pedigree and debut fifth at Kempton gives him something to build on

runners.name: Zotay Nzuri
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m Flat winner (RPR 69); sent off at 15-2 for last month's debut at Doncaster (2m, soft) but was well beaten in seventh of eight; considerable improvement is necessary today.
runners.comment: Seventh of eight on this month's debut at Doncaster and considerable improvement is needed

runners.name: Close To Me
runners.spotlight: Her dam was smart but this filly was tailed off at 40-1 on last month's debut at Newbury (2m, good to soft); she could do with settling better.
runners.comment: Her dam was smart but this filly was tailed off at 40-1 on last month's debut at Newbury

runners.name: Order Driven
runners.spotlight: Inexperience proved costly on debut at Haydock (1m7f, good to soft) three weeks ago, when hanging badly right on the first bend; finished well beaten at 9-1 and he may be best watched for now.
runners.comment: Inexperience proved costly on debut at Haydock this month; may be best watched for now

races.name: Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Handicap

runners.name: Doilookokay
runners.spotlight: Made a successful AW debut at Chelmsford last summer; no progress since, including two AW runs last month, but back to his winning mark and a revival isn't out of the question.
runners.comment: Chelmsford win last summer (5f) came off this mark; no progress since & needs full revival

runners.name: Mademoiselle Belle
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 14th attempt when scoring at Newcastle last month (cheekpieces first time); subsequent run easy to excuse and she was back on the up when third over 6f at Wolverhampton last week; runs off the same mark and should go well back at 5f.
runners.comment: Three solid efforts since cheekpieces went on; should make another bold bid

runners.name: Mythical Night
runners.spotlight: Weighted to turn the tables on Life After Love, who beat her a short head over C&D last month; however, over 2l to find with Mademoiselle Belle on her latest run (at Newcastle) and she's now 0-7.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden; best run came over C&D but held by Mademoiselle Belle on latest start

runners.name: Mad Dash
runners.spotlight: No improvement for the switch to handicaps on her last two starts, finishing behind Life After Love at Lingfield four weeks ago; edging down the weights but more is required.
runners.comment: No improvement for handicaps as yet; down in the weights but more is required

runners.name: Enter Sandman
runners.spotlight: He is being held back by a refusal to settle but has shown clear promise and his latest Newcastle run, where he was too free under early restraint, can be upgraded (made his move as the pace was picking up after a steady first 2f); Billy Loughnane on board tonight and he remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden but latest run suggested he could win races off this mark; more to come

runners.name: Life After Love
runners.spotlight: Beat Mythical Night by a short-head over C&D last month; went close at Lingfield next time but not quite so good over 6f at Kempton 24 days ago; she's still more likely to give her running than most.
runners.comment: C&D win last month has been followed by two fair runs in defeat; each-way shout

runners.name: Tenors Inn
runners.spotlight: Didn't score heavily on the consistency front as a 2yo but his peak effort, a close fourth at Beverley last August (5f, good to firm), came off a 3lb higher mark; absent since September; stable also runs Doilookokay.
runners.comment: Inconsistent 2yo but his peak effort came off a 3lb higher mark; stable runs two here

runners.name: Jane Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress for Jamie Osborne; prominent for a long way on her stable debut at Lingfield four weeks ago (first attempt at 5f); that was after a four-month break and she could last longer today.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on last month's stable debut; should last longer this time

runners.name: Storm Flag
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for Andrew Balding and she was well beaten on her stable debut at Newcastle 128 days ago (6f; 50-1); dropping down the weights but she is on a recovery mission.
runners.comment: Didn't progress for Andrew Balding and she was well beaten on stable debut 128 days ago

runners.name: Miss Boyd
runners.spotlight: Four runs as a 2yo, finishing well beaten in both AW starts; drops back to 5f for her seasonal return; improvement essential.
runners.comment: Well held in two AW runs as a 2yo; returns from six months off with a point to prove

runners.name: Independent Angel
runners.spotlight: Sent off at big prices and ran to a low level in six 2yo starts; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Sent off at big prices and ran to a low level in six 2yo starts; hard to recommend

runners.name: Bold Return
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward on her nursery debut at Beverley last August, racing wide but keeping on strongly for second in a 0-65 (form worked out); there are races to be won with her this year and she is considered despite her 224-day absence.
runners.comment: Good 2nd on her nursery debut at Beverley 224 days ago; more to come this year; respected

races.name: Midnite, Building The Future Of Betting Handicap

runners.name: St Faz
runners.spotlight: Strong in the betting and the finish when making a winning stable debut over C&D earlier in the month and he was 2lb wrong when defying a penalty at Lingfield (2m, Polytrack; Prince Quattro second) six days ago; goes unpenalised so he's effectively 2lb lower (and 6lb ahead of the handicapper) for this hat-trick bid; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Unbeaten for yard and chasing a hat-trick; races off lower than latest; hard to oppose

runners.name: Appier
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who arrives in form, his success at Wolverhampton (Tapeta) in January followed by solid third at Kempton (also about 2m, Polytrack) a fortnight later; ran well for second over extended 2m2f here three weeks ago and just as effective over this shorter trip; warrants respect.
runners.comment: Multiple winner who was second (about 2m2f) here latest; this trip fine and he's respected

runners.name: Prince Quattro
runners.spotlight: Registered a first Flat win when scoring over C&D in January and it was a creditable effort when chasing home St Faz at Lingfield (2m, Polytrack) six days ago; work to do if he's to turn the tables but still one of the more likely ones.
runners.comment: Chased home St Faz latest; hard to see a turnaround but still one of the more likely ones
runners.owner: Ursa Major Ltd & Hicky Parmar Racing

runners.name: Francesi
runners.spotlight: Joined Tony Carroll with a record of 1-44 but he's had a bumper winter, winning four times and finishing second on another four occasions; bounced back from a lesser effort when third at Wolverhampton on Tuesday but all runs for this yard were between 1m and 1m2f and this first go at 1m6f has the air of a hail mary.
runners.comment: Fine spell for this yard but has never won beyond 1m and this trip asks a big question

runners.name: Green Team
runners.spotlight: Dual winner at this trip last year, including on Tapeta, but he's been struggling to replicate the form of that last win in July despite a falling mark; due to drop a further 2lb.
runners.comment: Struggling to replicate form of latest win in July despite reduced mark; due 2lb drop

runners.name: Fleurman
runners.spotlight: Rated much higher in the past and he won for the first time since February 2023 at Kempton yesterday; has a rocky profile and this would be a quick return to action, but he can go well under a 4lb penalty if backing up last night's performance.
runners.comment: Won for the first time since February 2023 at Kempton last night; carries 4lb penalty

races.name: Bet Â£10 Get Â£40 With BetMGM Classified Stakes

runners.name: Sold Out
runners.spotlight: Career best when making all in Newcastle classified (1m) last month; hasn't matched that in three subsequent starts but he lost a shoe when back in a classified last time; probable contender with Billy Loughnane taking over.
runners.comment: Made all in Newcastle classified (1m) last month; lost a shoe latest; Loughnane takes over

runners.name: Chourmo
runners.spotlight: Has been attracting support since joining this yard; respectable fourth on penultimate start last month and he went fairly close when third (also 9.5f at Wolverhampton) ten days later; should handle the drop in trip and looks a player.
runners.comment: Getting it together over further for this yard and it was a close third last time; player

runners.name: Brother Dave
runners.spotlight: Running well here this year and he landed a classified in January; subsequently placed in two course handicaps (also 7f); didn't beat a rival in previous three starts at this sort of trip but still commands respect in current mood back in a classified.
runners.comment: 1m form (limited evidence) is poor but he's been in good nick over 7f this year; contender

runners.name: Spirit Of Bowland
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D handicap winner who's run respectably on his last five starts (all classifieds); beaten just 1l (7f; first-time blinkers, now removed) at Newcastle one week ago and he'll likely be on the premises once more.
runners.comment: Dual C&D handicap winner who's running respectably; close 5th latest; on the premises

runners.name: Sir Maxi
runners.spotlight: Last eight starts in classifieds and he didn't always get the breaks when third over C&D 12 days ago; that was one of his better recent efforts and he's firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Didn't always get the breaks when third over C&D 12 days ago; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Elettaria
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on Tapeta at Newcastle (all 7f; two classifieds), most recently in July; below her best in two starts this year but may have needed it when sixth of 12 in classified here (7f) a fortnight ago; probably stays this trip and she's not confidently ruled out.
runners.comment: May have needed it when sixth here (7f) latest; probably stays 1m and can't be ruled out

runners.name: Bitacora
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who came good for Daragh Bourke at third attempt, when landing a touch in 8.6f Wolverhampton classified (Tapeta) one year ago; down the field in a handicap back there on only subsequent start; watch the market on this first start since May.
runners.comment: Landed a touch in one of these a year ago; watch the market on this first start since May

runners.name: Dequinto
runners.spotlight: Broke his duck at the 22nd attempt when landing six-runner Newcastle classified (1m, Tapeta) under Myla Coppins last month but didn't built on that in two handicaps over that C&D subsequently; might do better back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Won 6-runner classified last month; not matched since; may do better back in this sphere

runners.name: Saved Lizzie
runners.spotlight: 6yo maiden mare; not solid on her form this winter but is one to note if the market speaks positively on this debut for John Butler.
runners.comment: 6yo maiden mare; not solid on her form this winter but watch the market on this yard debut

runners.name: Miss Helena
runners.spotlight: Struggled in two bumpers (wore eyeshields) in the autumn and three subsequent AW novice races (7f-9.4f; no headgear); had refitted eyeshields and first-time blinkers when 150-1 and beaten 8l in Newcastle classified (1m, Tapeta) last month; still fairly early days.
runners.comment: Modest form in a variety of events and headgear to date; still fairly early days

runners.name: Pop Favorite
runners.spotlight: Last win away from Newcastle was in 2021; slow starts have been holding him back so both classified fourths over C&D this month can arguably be upgraded; chance rests largely on the start.
runners.comment: Has ability to be involved but keeps blowing the start; chance if getting away on terms

runners.name: Clipsham Noble
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Brighton (1m, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) in June but he left Alice Haynes (same owner) not quite in the same form; makes classified debut after 112 days away with his new yard going great guns, however.
runners.comment: Left Alice Haynes below his best but has joined a yard going great guns; revival possible

races.name: Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Crest Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has shown more than enough to win a race; stepped up a little on his maiden/novice form when just held on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m) in September; subsequently gelded and sets a clear standard on this AW/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Close 2nd on h'cap debut in September; gelded since and sets the standard on AW debut

runners.name: Powder Monkey
runners.spotlight: 82,000gns breeze-up purchase who finished 6l second to a very promising sort on his debut at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta) 17 days ago; looks the part and is a firm contender to build on that.
runners.comment: 82,000gns breeze-up; 2nd to very promising sort on debut; can build on that; player

runners.name: King Of Berkshire
runners.spotlight: 145,000euros Camelot yearling; sent off favourite for debut when weakening third at Yarmouth (7f, soft) in September and he also didn't get home at York (1m, good) the following month; should do better this term; switches to AW for his reappearance.
runners.comment: Didn't get home in two turf starts last year; should do better this term; AW debut

runners.name: Brouhaha
runners.spotlight: By Coulsty; 80,000euros yearling; sixth foal; brother to US 1m/8.5f turf winner Glenall (including stakes), half-brother to four winners including Loch Carron (6f; RPR 81); dam unraced sister to 1m/1m2f winner Ningxai; didn't make the track for Ed Walker (same owners) and was also gelded/had wind surgery last year; betting should help ahead of this debut.
runners.comment: Unraced for Ed walker; wind surgery/gelded last year; watch the market on this debut

runners.name: Regulus Black
runners.spotlight: Should win races based on pedigree but he was 25-1 and beaten nearly 12l on his debut at Wolverhampton (about 1m, Tapeta) nine days ago; might need more time unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Should win races based on pedigree but he was 25-1 and beaten nearly 12l on recent debut

runners.name: Immediate Jewel
runners.spotlight: Beaten 15l when last of four on debut at Chelmsford last month; small step forward when a promoted fifth of eight at Chelmsford (also 1m, Polytrack) three weeks ago; stout pedigree (related to high-class chasers Observe and Shotgun Willy) and he's likely one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Has high-class staying chasers in the family and of more interest once handicapping

runners.name: My Dad Frank
runners.spotlight: Modest form when beaten 15l on his debut (Tapeta, 1m) last month and he was 400-1 when last of six over 1m4f in course novice a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Modest form on both starts and he would be a surprise winner
runners.owner: S McMillan F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Ghadra
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in sole start for Richard Hannon (6f) in October; changed hands for 7,000gns one week later and he finished last of seven when 125-1 for stable debut (5f, also here) a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: 125-1 and last of seven on stable debut here (5f) a fortnight ago

runners.name: Superfortress
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling who was bought unraced for 5,000gns in October 2024; outran his odds when 100-1 second at Newcastle (Tapeta) on debut one year later but didn't build on that in easy-ground turf novice (also 1m2f) a fortnight on; return to AW might help on this reappearance.
runners.comment: 100-1 but second on October's debut (1m2f, AW); not so good on easy turf next time

runners.name: Silver Splash
runners.spotlight: Dam won four times in Argentina including two Group 2s on dirt (1m); 40-1 shot on debut at Chelmsford (6f) but she made some late headway and was beaten only about 3l in fourth last month; that form isn't strong but she should know more this time and is a possible improver up in trip.
runners.comment: Only beaten 3l on debut (6f; 40-1); dam won G2s on dirt at 1m; possible improver
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Finlaggan
runners.spotlight: Ex-Godolphin 4yo related to Group winners; seventh of 14 at Fontainebleau (1m1f, good to soft) on sole start for Andre Fabre just under a year ago; bought for 10,000gns in October and subsequently gelded; has joined a yard that has done well with plenty of its French imports down the years and market clues are likely on this AW debut.
runners.comment: Mid-div sole start for Andre Fabre; bought for 10,000gns by shrewd connections; AW debut

races.name: Win Â£250,000 With BetMGM's Golden Goals Handicap

runners.name: Maximum Velocity
runners.spotlight: Out the back in his qualifying runs as a juvenile but much more like it when third of 12 at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta; Popty Ping no luck in sixth) on handicap debut three weeks ago; pulled hard and drifted in the market that day so likely he can build on that; player.
runners.comment: Good third on h'cap debut last time; reasons to think he can do better again; player

runners.name: Fickle McSelfish
runners.spotlight: Showed a good chunk of improvement when making virtually all to score comfortably at Newcastle (Tapeta, 7f) in first-time cheepieces one week ago; 5lb well in under his penalty but will need to get away cleanly from the widest stall if he's to repeat those tactics.
runners.comment: Big improver last week in added cheekpieces; drawn widest but 5lb well in under penalty

runners.name: Akrivos
runners.spotlight: Fourth on last three starts (1m-1m2f, Tapeta/Polytrack) including under Billy Loughnane at Lingfield three weeks ago; 2lb lower but this drop back to 7f asks a question.
runners.comment: Fourth under Billy Loughnane over 1m2f latest; 2lb lower but drop back to 7f is a query

runners.name: Quick Quasar
runners.spotlight: All starts at 7f; best of three qualifying runs came when 9-2 and fifth of seven at Wolverhampton (Tapeta) on debut in December; went backwards on Polytrack afterwards but now makes handicap debut back on Tapeta having been gelded; cheekpieces also added and he's bred to be better than this opening mark.
runners.comment: Best of three qualifying runs came on Tapeta; has been gelded; watch market on h'cap debut

runners.name: Tickettothestars
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in nine starts on turf/Tapeta, including five handicaps, though she didn't run at all badly when fifth in 5f course maiden (125-1) a fortnight ago; tackles 7f for the first time.
runners.comment: Yet to place in nine starts; solid effort in course maiden last time but that was over 5f

runners.name: Mereside Princess
runners.spotlight: 0-8 and she's been fairly consistent without progressing in her five handicaps; will likely run her race again but others probably have more scope for improvement.
runners.comment: Consistent but not progressing in h'caps; likely runs her race but others of more interest

runners.name: Popty Ping
runners.spotlight: Opened her account in a first-time visor with narrow win over C&D in December and some fair efforts since, including when she was unlucky not to finish much closer when sixth at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta; Maximum Velocity third) three weeks ago; just 1lb higher than her winning mark and she has to be considered.
runners.comment: Unlucky last time and is just 1lb above her last winning mark; has to be considered

runners.name: Forceful Lady
runners.spotlight: More miss than hit in handicaps but her second of nine at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta) in November would give her a squeak off this reduced mark.
runners.comment: Unconvincing but November's second (7f, Tapeta) gives her a squeak off this reduced mark

runners.name: Sirhana
runners.spotlight: 100-1 when she didn't get a totally clear shot on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton last month but it was an undistinguished effort when last at Newcastle (also 7f, Tapeta; 40-1) three weeks ago; others look more likely.
runners.comment: Undistinguished effort when last at Newcastle (also 7f, Tapeta; 40-1) three weeks ago

runners.name: Serragasso
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 25l in a Lingfield maiden and novice (1m) around the turn of the year but appeared to take a good step forward when 200-1 and fourth of sixth at Chelmsford (7f, also Polytrack; had been gelded) 19 days ago; probably needs more on this handicap debut but it's very early days.
runners.comment: Appeared to step forward when 200-1 fourth latest; h'cap debut and may be more to come

runners.name: Saxon Grace
runners.spotlight: No wins from seven starts in Ireland for Claire O'Connell but a good effort at Dundalk (Polytrack) on penultimate start for that yard showed she acts on AW; subject of a gamble on her stable debut at Wolverhampton (also 6f, Tapeta; drawn wide) but she wilted late having had to put in an early shift; draw has not been overly kind again so interesting to see how she goes in the market this time.
runners.comment: Gambled on when making stable debut last time; drawn wide again but needs a market watch

runners.name: Kwidsin
runners.spotlight: Some promise, despite hanging badly right, when fifth of nine at Musselburgh (7f; 15-2) on debut in August; unfortunately that trait got the better of him again when last at Ayr (6f, also good) eight days later and he was never going when 80-1 and beaten 20l at Pontefract (good to soft) the following month; switches to AW for this seasonal/handicap debut; market may guide.
runners.comment: Didn't build on fair debut; switches to AW for seasonal/h'cap debut; market helpful

races.name: Read Meg Nicholls' Blog At betmgm.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Horwich
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard when down the field on his stable debut here (7f) two weeks ago; drops to 6f with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Pulled hard when down the field on his recent stable debut here (7f); bounce back needed

runners.name: Wyle Cop
runners.spotlight: Promise as a 2yo; gelded prior to his reappearance at Wolverhampton 23 days ago, a race where he made all and won well enough; more to do back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Made all on his reappearance at Wolverhampton this month; more needed in handicap company

runners.name: Start Me Up
runners.spotlight: Promising second over C&D last October, his second racecourse start; no better than midfield in three runs in Dubai over the winter; this should be less demanding and he's not fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D last October; modest form in Dubai this winter; this should be easier

runners.name: Numero Vingt
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in his one turf start as a 2yo; gelded afterwards and made a winning reappearance when dead-heating in a Chelmsford maiden six weeks ago (6f); narrowly defied a penalty at Lingfield 13 days later and that form has been boosted with the second and fourth winning since; needs more to defy this mark but he is willing and could progress further.
runners.comment: 2-2 on AW this year and latest Lingfield win has been franked; should have more to come

runners.name: Fair Y Isle
runners.spotlight: She has improved with each start, getting off the mark in a 6f fillies' novice at Wolverhampton nine weeks ago; open to improvement but others look to have greater depth to their form.
runners.comment: Progress run by run, off the mark dropped to 6f latest; unexposed but needs a step forward

runners.name: Harswell Angel
runners.spotlight: Improved to win a 6f novice at Redcar in October on his third start; struggled on his nursery debut three weeks later but the heavy ground may not have been to his liking; a career-best effort will be required if he is to take this after his absence.
runners.comment: 6f turf win on 3rd start; ground perhaps unsuitable when last seen; needs a personal best

runners.name: Riley Rocks
runners.spotlight: Shaped well amidst evident inexperience here on debut in December (5f); duly stepped forward when winning a 6f novice at Newcastle 12 weeks ago; four of the seven beaten runners have won since and he moves into handicaps with the potential for further progress.
runners.comment: Confirmed debut promise (5f) by beating four subsequent winners over 6f latest; unexposed

races.name: Midnite: Built For 2026 Not 2006 Handicap

runners.name: Insuspense
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 12 starts but she has finished second on her last four outings, including over C&D; no obvious reason why she wouldn't give her running once again.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden but she's finished second on her last four starts; should be involved again

runners.name: Winchurch
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his 2025 best and he hasn't always enjoyed much luck this winter; creditable fifth at Lingfield (6f) when last seen in January; not a solid option but has the ability to feature.
runners.comment: On a lowly mark and hasn't always had the rub of the green this winter; can't discount
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Ramon Di Loria
runners.spotlight: Won twice (6f/7f) in February and he was a good second to a thriving rival over C&D in his bid for a hat-trick; beaten off a lower mark twice since and more minor money perhaps his best hope.
runners.comment: Two wins in February; fully effective over C&D; place money perhaps his best hope

runners.name: Good Earth
runners.spotlight: Ended his losing run in a C&D handicap in January; only 1lb higher today but his form hasn't been at the same level in two runs since (met trouble on first occasion); others look more persuasive.
runners.comment: Chance if back to his C&D-winning form in January; latest effort needs bettering though

runners.name: Dragon God
