runners.spotlight: Ran okay on his stable debut at Kempton 24 days ago and that run should have sharpened him up; can win races for his new stable but may need a bit more weight off his back.
runners.comment: Ran okay on stable debut 24 days ago but he may need a bit more help from the handicapper

runners.name: Beelzebub
runners.spotlight: He has never been prolific but lurks on a dangerous mark and his latest unlucky-in-running fourth over C&D served notice he was ready to strike; high on the list.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark and latest unlucky fourth strongly hinted his turn was near

runners.name: Ziggys Angel
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to handicaps when winning a 7f event at Newcastle last September; better form when third in a Class 5 handicap over C&D in November; had wind surgery after a weak finishing effort here (7f) 88 days ago; not fully exposed and he commands respect.
runners.comment: 7f win on handicap debut and he has also run well over C&D; had a wind op since latest run

runners.name: Rock Of England
runners.spotlight: Returns from a five-month break on a competitive mark but still seeking a first AW success and this perhaps not the day to catch him.
runners.comment: On a fair mark but he is returning from a break and may come on for this; 0-5 on AW too

runners.name: Travel Agent
runners.spotlight: Ran out the ready winner of a 6f handicap at Lingfield (well supported at big odds) when dropped into Class 6 company last August; not so good in two subsequent runs but dropping back into this grade will help; one to consider starting out for Adam Kirby.
runners.comment: Impressed in his only other Class 6 outing; one to consider starting out for Adam Kirby

runners.name: Blackjack
runners.spotlight: Conditions to suit and this mark shouldn't be beyond him; he does need to bounce back from a couple of low-key runs in February though.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit but he has to bounce back from two low-key efforts in February

races.name: ECIS Health Conditional Jockeys' Training Series Handicap Hurdle (Racing Excellence Initiative)

runners.name: Timeforarum
runners.spotlight: Has run well on good/good to soft but reportedly benefited from switch to heavy ground when bouncing back to form to win by 7l at Ffos Las last month; subsequent 5lb rise is not excessive but it's hard to predict whether he'll be in the right mood again.
runners.comment: Bounced back to form with heavy-ground win last month but is not easy to predict

runners.name: Nobody Told Me
runners.spotlight: Stayed on for very respectable fourth at Hereford (2m3f, good) this month, his second run in a visor, but is yet to be placed after 11 hurdling starts and needs to find something extra today.
runners.comment: Latest run was very respectable but he's yet to be placed after 11 hurdling starts

runners.name: Kings Order
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when 100-1 for last month's handicap hurdle debut here (2m3f, soft; raced too freely) but came second in good-ground Irish point last spring and was running well before falling in good-ground novice hurdle in the autumn; back on forecast decent ground today; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 100-1 for handicap debut but can do much better now back on faster ground

runners.name: This Sway
runners.spotlight: Rewarded heavy market support when winning by 5l at Southwell (2m, soft) this month, his sixth start over hurdles; went up 7lb for that but should be okay on the forecast faster ground here and is open to more improvement if aided by first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Landed big gamble at Southwell this month, his sixth hurdling start; cheekpieces added now

runners.name: Cogital
runners.spotlight: Won small-field Flat handicap last summer but ended 2025 in poor form over hurdles and needs to have been refreshed by three-month break.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in poor form and needs to have been refreshed by three-month break

runners.name: Dish Of The Day
runners.spotlight: Disappointed when made favourite at Taunton (2m3f, heavy) last month, after a layoff, but is now 6lb lower than when placed in first-time cheekpieces at Stratford (2m2f, good to soft) just over a year ago, and can rebound on the less testing ground here.
runners.comment: Disappointed on heavy ground last month but returns to quicker conditions on good mark

runners.name: Silent Revolution
runners.spotlight: Useful in his prime; badly out of sorts for Alison Thorpe last year (five runs) but makes stable debut from a very dangerous mark and with the trip/ground suitable; well worth keeping an eye on in the betting.
runners.comment: Out of sorts last year but makes stable debut from a very dangerous mark; watch market

races.name: Glidevale Protect Handicap Chase

runners.name: Moor End
runners.spotlight: Switched to chasing this season and ran well on first four starts, before an early unseat 12 days ago; all those were over about 2m4f and two attempts at this sort of trip last term as a hurdler left his stamina unproven.
runners.comment: Ran well in first four chases, before early unseat latest; all those were over about 2m4f
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Aj Mutch & Mrs Ej Bishop

runners.name: Just In A Bit
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage maiden who's been second or third in handicaps on all three chase outings, absent 590 days after first of those; rallied strongly when third of seven at Leicester (2m7f, soft) one month ago, which suggests that this step back up in trip is very much in order; one to consider.
runners.comment: Rallied strongly for 3rd of seven at Leicester (2m7f, soft) one month ago; one to consider

runners.name: Yes And Yes
runners.spotlight: Won here (2m3f, good) last March off just 1lb lower but produced two low-key efforts this season (May and October) before a fall at Ludlow (five out, work to do) four weeks ago; stamina unproven; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won here (2m3f, good) last March off just 1lb lower; low-key efforts this season
runners.owner: David W Jagger & Mrs Belinda Clarke

runners.name: Night Jet
runners.spotlight: Won chase debut in February 2025 and back to form with blinkers enlisted when second on last two outings, but all three of those runs were over 2m and there is plenty to prove judged on his attempts at 2m4f/3m; cheekpieces return and their previous usage includes that one win.
runners.comment: Back to form with blinkers enlisted when second on last two outings, but those were at 2m

runners.name: Montmartin
runners.spotlight: Just six runs in more than three years; placed over about 3m in his first two handicaps (hurdle/chase) but bled from the nose in January (first run for 288 days) and pulled up; tongue tied last two starts; could still have potential if all is well with him.
runners.comment: Bled from nose in January (first run for 288 days) and pulled up; potential if all's well

runners.name: Bridget Mary
runners.spotlight: 2-6 in handicap chases, the latest win by 10l at Bangor (3m, good; 8lb higher today) last April; creditable second there when returning in October but backward steps on her two starts since; latest (Windsor on New Year's Day) is a worry.
runners.comment: Creditable second when returning in October but backward steps on her two starts since

runners.name: Benignitas
runners.spotlight: Sent chasing on last three starts, with minor honours over the extended 2m3f at Ffos Las in two of those; however, she is now 0-15 under rules, in which sphere she is unraced beyond an extended 2m5f; cheekpieces (tried twice on Flat in 2020, well beaten) are restored.
runners.comment: 0-15 under rules, in which sphere unraced beyond extended 2m5f; last cheekpieces in 2020

runners.name: Badentaggart
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo with bits of modest form, including in two of his four starts blinkered; weakened right out of it when upped to about 3m on latest outing, albeit on heavy going; two of his winning siblings have stayed.
runners.comment: Weakened right out of it when upped to about 3m on latest outing, albeit on heavy

runners.name: Tom Taggle
runners.spotlight: Career highlight when winning a 15-runner handicap hurdle here (extended 2m7f, good) last April off 4lb lower; easily best of three runs this season was also his only one at this sort of trip (penultimate start) and he's one to keep an eye on now back up in trip on chase debut.
runners.comment: Hurdle win here (extended 2m7f, good) last April; back up in trip on today's chase debut

runners.name: Flemenstide
runners.spotlight: Below form in 2022-23 on final two starts for Paul Nicholls and back from a mammoth absence with two uninspiring chase runs this year; cheekpieces (tried twice in 2022, ran well first occasion) return.
runners.comment: Back from a mammoth absence with two uninspiring chase runs this year; cheekpieces return

races.name: Ashcroft Services Mares' "National Hunt" Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: The Procrastinator
runners.spotlight: Won soft-ground junior bumper at Doncaster in November and was also successful against own age-group on last month's hurdle debut at Hereford (2m, heavy); managed only third when warm favourite at Wetherby (2m, soft; raced too freely) four weeks ago and needs to prove herself on the forecast faster ground here but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Bumper/hurdle winner against other juveniles; player if handling today's faster ground

runners.name: Just A Glance
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer who was second in 17-runner Aintree bumper (good) on rules debut last May and made pleasing start to hurdling career last month, winning Hereford maiden (2m, soft) from the front; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Won Hereford maiden on last month's hurdling debut and has obvious claims here

runners.name: Evening Tess
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to bumper/hurdle winner Hillfinch; dam fair 2m7f/3m hurdle winner; represents a top stable and does not have a daunting standard to reach on debut; market may guide.
runners.comment: Out of a fair 2m7f/3m hurdle winner; represents a top stable; makes debut here

runners.name: Skiptothepost
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in all five points, the latest in April 2024, and also when 300-1 for last month's rules debut at Ffos Las (2m4f, heavy); drops in trip and sports first-time hood/refitted tongue-tie here.
runners.comment: Pulled up in all five points and also when 300-1 for last month's hurdle debut

runners.name: Hollysaway
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam runner-up in a point, half-sister to winning chaser Bally Lagan; wears a tongue-tie on debut; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Fourth foal from a point runner-up; probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Lakeview Lass
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to very useful jumper Midnight It Is but struggled in both her bumpers and can't be recommended on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Struggled in both her bumpers and can't be recommended on hurdling debut

runners.name: Miss Denver
runners.spotlight: With good stable and has good pedigree but was well beaten in soft-ground bumper last season and pulled up when reappearing in 2m3f Doncaster hurdle (also on soft) in the autumn.
runners.comment: With good yard and has good pedigree but well beaten on both starts (bumper/hurdle)

runners.name: Lady Dapple Rose
runners.spotlight: Second of four on hurdling debut at Leicester (1m7f, soft) in November but the value of that form is open to question and she was safely held on her next two outings; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Second of four on hurdling debut but safely held twice since; others look stronger

races.name: Stephens Industries Epdm Flat Roofing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gingerbred
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice in December (2m7f and near 3m3f) but right out of form since, way behind Lightonthewing last time; headgear first time.
runners.comment: 2nd twice in December (2m7f and near 3m3f) but right out of form since; first headgear

runners.name: River Voyage
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with two wins over hurdles and he outstayed his rivals to snatch another at Ffos Las (3m, heavy; under Toby McCain-Mitchell) five weeks ago in his fifth handicap chase; back up 3lb but, unraced beyond 3m, looks a strong candidate for long-distance races and therefore may be able to raise his game further.
runners.comment: Snatched a 3m win at Ffos Las (heavy) latest; strong candidate for long-distance races

runners.name: To Be Sure
runners.spotlight: Generally in much better form last March-October than in his three starts this winter; no impact in his four races beyond 3m2f and the bottom line seems to be that he last won a race in April 2023.
runners.comment: No impact in his four races beyond 3m2f and he last won a race in April 2023

runners.name: Stellar Stream
runners.spotlight: Last won in the North Wales National at Bangor (extended 3m5f, heavy) in February 2024 with first-time cheekpieces; best effort this term was back in November (four races since) but he's down the weights, back chasing and some headgear returns after two hurdles races without; yard going well too.
runners.comment: Best effort this term was back in November but there are reasons why he could revive

runners.name: Esperti
runners.spotlight: Huge fall in the weights preceded a fruitful 2025, including two wins from off the pace late in the year, but he's not been competitive in his two starts in 2026; going beyond 3m2f is something new.
runners.comment: Two wins from off the pace late in 2025; not competitive in his two starts in 2026

runners.name: Gold Emery
runners.spotlight: All starts on softer than good; no win from 15 attempts over fences but runner-up in seven, including off this mark when he tried an extended 3m6f in the North Wales National at Bangor last time; probably the most likely to give his running and get seriously involved.
runners.comment: No win from 15 runs over fences but runner-up in seven, including extended 3m6f last time

runners.name: Lightonthewing
runners.spotlight: Often front-runner; ridden by Ciaran O'Shea on last four starts; 7-22 in chases (one of his hurdles wins was this track) and those wins include his last two completed starts in this sphere, the latest at Exeter (3m, good to soft) three weeks ago; this trip is unknown territory but he ran well over an extended 3m4f at Stratford in 2024; has to be in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Won his last two completed chases, including latest; ran well in a 3m4f race in 2024

runners.name: Handin Manypockets
runners.spotlight: 5yo whose first two runs under rules were more promising than the two since; latest was chase debut here (extended 2m7f, soft) in which he plugged on for third of 11 but was beaten 18l in that handicap; now tries a marathon trip and first-time headgear, for yard among the winners on Tuesday.
runners.comment: 5yo; not so promising in his two handicaps; tries a marathon trip and first headgear

runners.name: Amateur
runners.spotlight: A regular in long-distance chases, with an excellent record at Ffos Las but pulled up on all four appearances over these fences (twice in Welsh National); he's now a 13yo with no win since May 2024 (his only race under Edward Vaughan) but some ability remained last spring when last seen and he's accelerating down the weights.
runners.comment: 13yo with no win since May 2024 but some ability remained last spring when last seen

races.name: DB Roofing Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bells Of Ufford
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but came second over C&D (good) last spring and made pleasing seasonal debut when fourth at Uttoxeter (2m, good; first-time cheekpieces) in October; well beaten on soft ground twice since but gets back on a quicker surface today and is on a good mark now; tongue-tie refitted.
runners.comment: On good mark now and sure to be suited by this return to forecast quicker ground

runners.name: Inion Tiogair
runners.spotlight: Drew clear to win by over 9l over C&D (soft) in February, opening her hurdling account at the 16th attempt; not sure to be ideally suited by today's forecast quicker ground but her 5lb higher mark ought to be manageable, and she certainly didn't run badly on heavy going at Ffos Las this month.
runners.comment: Emphatic C&D winner last month; player if coping with forecast quicker ground today

runners.name: Sailing By
runners.spotlight: Showed only minor promise on first three hurdling starts (2m-2m3f) but was placed in 13-runner course bumper on debut in November and has scope for improvement on this handicap hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Placed in a course bumper; low-key start to hurdling career but might improve in handicaps
runners.owner: J M Dare T Hamlin J W Snook

runners.name: Locked Down Lad
runners.spotlight: About 21l behind Inion Tiogair when sixth of 11 over C&D (soft) six weeks ago and now 0-11 over hurdles but ought to be much better suited by the less testing conditions today, and is now only 1lb higher than when second in a big field over C&D (good to soft) in the autumn.
runners.comment: 21l behind Inion Tiogair when sixth here last month but this faster going will suit better

runners.name: Nancefordshore
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in both Irish points last spring and over C&D on rules debut in December but made steady late headway to finish sixth at Hereford (2m/2m3f) on last two outings and further progress is possible in handicaps.
runners.comment: Made steady headway to finish sixth in last two qualifying races; improvement possible

runners.name: Luwdvig
runners.spotlight: Came good over hurdles at fifth attempt, with clearcut success at Lingfield (2m3f, soft) in January; well beaten (albeit in better race than this) last month, though, and may not be ideally suited by this quicker ground.
runners.comment: Soft-ground winner in January but well beaten last time; quicker conditions a worry here
runners.owner: The Ridgeway Racing For Fun Partnership

runners.name: Le Fabuleux Bucks
runners.spotlight: Progressed in novice company over the winter and was good third at Lingfield (2m, heavy) on latest outing; Sean Bowen is booked for this handicap debut; contender if new trip-and-ground combination suits.
runners.comment: Progressed in novice company during the winner; up in trip with Sean Bowen booked here

runners.name: Laurier Des Obeaux
runners.spotlight: Displayed clear ability in all three qualifying races, most recently when fourth of six behind useful opposition at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) last month, and should have some improvement to come in handicaps.
runners.comment: Fourth in good Newbury novice last month, and can improve in handicaps

runners.name: The Two Harrys
runners.spotlight: Ran as though something was amiss on last month's handicap debut and needs to put that behind him but his Plumpton second in December (2m4f, soft) was encouraging and he probably still has potential.
runners.comment: Ran as though something was amiss on handicap debut; may still deliver on earlier promise

runners.name: Can You Hear Me
runners.spotlight: Ahead of Laurier Des Obeaux when second in Lingfield novice (2m3f, good to soft) in November but safely held on both outings since, more recently on last month's handicap debut (raced too freely); hood added here.
runners.comment: Second in novice in November but safely held on handicap debut (raced too freely); hood on

runners.name: Hidden Target
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous well well-held sixth of 15 on handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m4f, soft) two months ago but remains unexposed, and the evidence from his qualifying races suggests today's faster ground will suit better.
runners.comment: Never looked dangerous on handicap debut but today's faster ground may suit better

runners.name: Izzys Grey
runners.spotlight: 0-13 over hurdles; placed twice at Bangor (2m7f, good) last spring but pulled up four consecutive times since; big leap of faith required to support him here.
runners.comment: Placed twice last spring but pulled up four consecutive times since; too risky

races.name: NFRC South West Roofing Industry Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Blue In The West
runners.spotlight: 0-20 over hurdles and has some quirks but returned from break with two good efforts this month, latterly when second of 17 at Exeter (2m7f, good to soft); Sean Bowen rides today; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Not straightforward but has posted two good efforts this month and Sean Bowen rides today

runners.name: Northern Symphonie
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner (once over C&D) on good ground for Keiran Burke last spring but pulled up on stable debut in December and came last of six at Wetherby (2m3f, soft) last month; needs to get career back on track.
runners.comment: Will be suited by a return to good ground but needs to get her career back on track

runners.name: Bredon Hill Dart
runners.spotlight: Not always one to rely upon heavily but won at Exeter (2m5f, heavy) last month and stayed on for third (just behind Blue In The West) in big field there (2m7f, good to soft) nine days ago; could have a say.
runners.comment: Not always reliable but won at Exeter last month and was recently third in big field there
runners.owner: R W And Mrs Mitchell D V Gardner

runners.name: Clondaw Royale
runners.spotlight: Exploited reduced mark under Benjamin Macey at Wetherby (3m, soft) in January and last month's heavy-ground third was perfectly respectable; also suited by much quicker surfaces; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Exploited reduced mark at Wetherby in January and this quicker ground will also suit

runners.name: No Rematch
runners.spotlight: Returned from layoff with quite encouraging C&D fourth (albeit well held in the end) on soft ground last month but is getting long in the tooth and today's faster ground is probably not in his favour; headgear refitted.
runners.comment: Ran okay here last month, after a layoff, but slow ground seems to suit this veteran best

runners.name: Colonial Empire
runners.spotlight: Close second of 16 at Taunton (3m, good) in December, after a break, and again ran well in defeat at Catterick (3m1f, soft) in January; more exposed than some of these but still respected.
runners.comment: More exposed than some of these but ran well in defeat on last two appearances

runners.name: Devon Dude
runners.spotlight: Disappointing over fences this season (four attempts) and never really recovered from bad, first-flight error when back over hurdles last month; on dangerous mark for this first venture beyond about 2m6f, though, and has first-time cheekpieces and Harry Cobden enlisted; interesting.
runners.comment: Needs to get career back on track but has new cheekpieces and Harry Cobden enlisted

runners.name: The Italian Fox
runners.spotlight: Remains unexposed after only four hurdling starts but was just a respectable fifth of nine when upped to 3m for handicap debut at Taunton (good) 12 weeks ago and needs a better performance here.
runners.comment: Still unexposed over hurdles but was just a respectable fifth of nine on handicap debut

runners.name: Richardson
runners.spotlight: Slow-ground specialist who won twice over 2m3f (once here) in February 2025; should be fine over this trip but is yet to strike form this season (three runs; bled last time) and there may not be enough rain for him today.
runners.comment: Slow-ground specialist who is yet to strike form after three runs this season

runners.name: Teorie
runners.spotlight: Out of form on slow ground during the winter but his recent fifth at Wincanton (3m, good to soft) was a step back in the right direction, and he might be seen to better effect if the ground is genuinely good here.
runners.comment: Took step back in right direction when back on better ground this month; not ruled out

runners.name: Crystello
runners.spotlight: Suited by step up to 3m when winning on handicap debut at Musselburgh (3m, soft) last month, drawing clear of the others with a subsequent winner; only 4lb higher here and should be fine on the faster ground; big player.
runners.comment: Clear of the others with a subsequent winner when scoring on handicap debut; big player

runners.name: Colby Mayfield
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who battled on for third when upped to 2m6f for recent handicap debut at Stratford (soft); needs to improve here but could plausibly be better suited by the quicker ground.
runners.comment: Battled on for third when upped to 2m6f for recent handicap debut; needs to build on that

runners.name: Jacks Jury
runners.spotlight: Back in prime form when scoring at Ffos Las (2m4f, soft; first-time cheekpieces) on stable debut ten days ago; only due a 4lb rise but completes under 7lb penalty here, and 2m7f on quicker ground should also suit.
runners.comment: Won recent stable debut at Ffos Las; only due 4lb rise but takes chance under 7lb penalty

runners.name: Lion Of The Desert
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles; belied 50-1 odds when placed at Ludlow (2m5f, soft) in February, his third run for Grace Harris, but didn't really build on that effort last time; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Placed at big odds two starts ago but didn't really build on that effort last time

runners.name: Rodney Bay
runners.spotlight: Had modest 2-44 strike-rate under rules when based in Ireland and was pulled up in a chase on latest outing five weeks ago; not an easy one to weigh up but might have been perked up by recent stable switch; wears first-time cheekpieces; well worth a glance in the market.
runners.comment: Had modest strike-rate in Ireland but worth a market check before this stable debut

races.name: SIG Roofing Ltd Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Valiant Soldier
runners.spotlight: Fifth of ten at Wincanton (heavy; 11-1) in January and fifth of 12 at the same track (good to soft; 33-1) three weeks ago; while that showed some ability, more is surely needed to win this.
runners.comment: While both his Wincanton races showed some ability, more is surely needed to win this

runners.name: Trigg
runners.spotlight: Won at Worcester (good to soft) in September; found one too good when 8-11 under 7lb claimer for a six-runner race at Sedgefield (soft) in December but still brings potential and has joined major owners.
runners.comment: Won at Worcester and second at Sedgefield; still brings potential; has joined major owners

runners.name: Stick To The Board
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros 3yo; fifth foal; half-brother to bumper, 1m7f hurdle and 2m3f chase winner Belle The Lioness (pretty useful); dam unplaced close relation to high-class 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner Monalee; challenging when he fell at the final fence in Irish maiden point (yielding to soft) in November; sold for Â£215,000 five days later and the other two principals in that Irish race have since been useful bumper winners; one to note for powerful connections.
runners.comment: Â£215,000 buy after his point fall; first two in that Irish race are useful bumper winners

runners.name: Shuil Rocco
runners.spotlight: 14,500euros foal, 24,000euros 3yo; eighth foal; half-brother to winners He Fitz In (2m5f-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Shuil Luas (point); dam point winner, half-sister to useful 2m3f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Ballycullen Boy; ran in two Irish points (good) last May, third of five over 2m4f on second occasion; trainer is 7-29 in bumpers.
runners.comment: Ran in two Irish points (good) last May, 3rd of five latterly; trainer is 7-29 in bumpers

runners.name: Roccobye
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to fair winners Blue Beach (bumper/2m4f hurdle) and Pique Rock (2m3f-2m5f hurdle/chase); dam placed in bumper and 2m-2m5f hurdle races, half-sister to bumper and smart 2m hurdle/chase winner I'msingingtheblues; trainer has had one bumper win this term and the market can guide.
runners.comment: Trainer has had one bumper win this term and the market can guide
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Jumping And Greatrex

runners.name: Egor Flight
runners.spotlight: 15,000euros 3yo; fifth foal; brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Drop Kick, half-brother to winners Helly Hansum (3m hurdle) and Nice To Meet (3m chase); dam 1m4f AW winner (RPR 67); trainer's bumper runners are rare.
runners.comment: 15,000euros 3yo; dam has produced jump winners; trainer's bumper runners are rare

runners.name: Lord Du Gouet
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam sister to smart 2m-3m hurdle/chase Val De Ferbet, half-sister to several winners; won an Irish maiden point (soft to heavy) and sold for Â£70,000 six days later in November; trainer is making quite a splash in bumpers and this 5yo needs a close check.
runners.comment: Won an Irish maiden point (soft to heavy) and sold for Â£70,000 six days later in November

runners.name: Telluswhen
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 2m3f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Hidden History (fairly useful); dam 1m6f-2m Flat (RPR 88) and 2m hurdle winner, sister to winners Bollin Julie (2m4f/3m hurdle) and Two B's (2m hurdle); rare bumper runner for his trainer.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 2m3f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Hidden History (fairly useful)

runners.name: Dukeboxrock
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m4f/3m hurdle winner in the mud; 15-2, faded to be tailed-off fifth of seven at Doncaster (soft) two months ago, so looks a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: 15-2, faded to be tailed-off fifth of seven at Doncaster (soft) two months ago

runners.name: Mariniere
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 22l at Newton Abbot (good; for Keiran Burke) last June and Wincanton (good to soft) three weeks ago, so no form claims yet.
runners.comment: Beaten about 22l at Newton Abbot last June and Wincanton (new yard) three weeks ago

races.name: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Blue Deveron
runners.spotlight: Has taken a good hold in 5f events but kept on both times and his debut form, namely close second at Southwell, helps to set the standard in this field; settling better would boost his chance upped to 6f.
runners.comment: Helps to set the standard; settling better would boost his chance upped to 6f

runners.name: Militzie
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly who drops back in trip having weakened over 7f at Wolverhampton; may confirm the promise of her debut effort, namely fourth over C&D when she was the sole newcomer in a field of eight.
runners.comment: Drops back in trip; may confirm the promise of her debut effort, namely fourth over C&D

runners.name: Moscow Power
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since Nottingham defeat last June when failing to back up the apparent promise of his Haydock effort (6f) behind two Godolphin runners; not a solid option but the debut form is respectable.
runners.comment: Not a solid option but his debut form is respectable; gelded since last run

runners.name: The Untamed
runners.spotlight: Bated Breath gelding; dam 6f 2yo winner (on second start; RPR 95) for his connections, sister to Group-placed 5f/6f 2yo scorer Parsley; one of several newcomers in the field; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Dam 6f winner (on second start) for his connections; heed the market signals

runners.name: Romeos Guardian
runners.spotlight: 48,000euros yearling; by Sergei Prokofiev and first foal of 7f AW winner who is a half-sister to two US dirt scorers, from the family of 5f/6f Group 1 winner Lady Aurelia; bred to take well to artificial surfaces; interesting debutant.
runners.comment: 48,000euros yearling; bred to take well to artificial surfaces; interesting debutant

runners.name: Hever Beauty
runners.spotlight: Land Force filly; sibling to seven winners, notably George William (7f/1m including AW; 105), but cost only 1,000gns as a foal and wears a hood on debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Cost only 1,000gns as a foal an wears hood on debut; best watched

runners.name: Admirable Fellow
runners.spotlight: Aclaim gelding; first foal of unraced half-sister to winners Ananya (useful 7f 2yo) and Itizzit (1m), from family of 1m Group 3 scorer Whazzis; the betting should be instructive as regards this Prescott debutant.
runners.comment: Aclaim gelding; the betting should be instructive as regards this Prescott debutant

runners.name: Sargent Dennis
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling; Invincible Army half-brother to 6f AW winner Badosa (RPR 62); dam 5f AW 2yo scorer (59); this newcomer may show promise with the surface likely to suit.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; relate to two AW winners; may show promise

runners.name: Hallandale Beach
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns foal, 55,000gns yearling; Havana Grey half-brother to five winners, notably Art Of America (1m-10.6f including AW; RPR 79); Amo Racing runner who looks one of the better types among the newcomers.
runners.comment: 55,000gns yearling; by Havana Grey; Amo Racing runner who looks one of the better types

runners.name: Fenay Bridge
runners.spotlight: Modest fourth at Newcastle kept to 5f, again recording an RPR of 43; this extra furlong needs to prompt improvement.
runners.comment: Modest claims on her 5f efforts

runners.name: My Mate Mackley
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced colt who brings the worst form and thus needs to improve sharply for dropping about two furlongs in distance.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form; drops in distance

races.name: All Weather Handicap

runners.name: Combustion
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism in Class 5 event at Kempton last time, taking record this year to 3-3 and overall strike-rate over 6f on AW to 7-9; he is 1-1 at Chelmsford; progressive sort who brings excellent stats and commands respect upped further in grade.
runners.comment: Scored at Kempton last time, taking record over 6f on AW to 7-9; progressive and respected

runners.name: Supreme King
runners.spotlight: Unlucky fifth, getting no sort of run, in the Kempton event won by Lazzar when bidding for a 6f four-timer; treated as still in form and still well handicapped (mark is unchanged); remains of interest at this level.
runners.comment: Unlucky fifth to Lazzar at Kempton when bidding for four-timer; treated as still in form

runners.name: Lazzar
runners.spotlight: Prevailed narrowly under Donagh Murphy at Kempton to make it four wins from his last five attempts over 6f; possibly hasn't reached his ceiling over this trip yet; 1-1 at Chelmsford; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Prevailed narrowly at Kempton to make it four wins from last five attempts over 6f

runners.name: Ziggys Condor
runners.spotlight: Second-place finish at Newcastle last time put two below-par efforts behind him; by no means certain to go one better, as he's an infrequent winner and takes on a few rivals who are better in that respect.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner; takes on a few rivals who are better in that respect

runners.name: Hunky Dory
runners.spotlight: Took well to first-time cheekpieces in 6f handicap at Southwell, opening his account; spoiled the follow-up attempt by racing too freely; sold out of Mick Appleby's yard for 30,000gns since; market may offer guidance in relation to the change of scenery.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell on penultimate start for Mick Appleby; debut for new yard

runners.name: Hectic
runners.spotlight: May be nicely primed with Newcastle reappearance under his belt; however, he's not in the ideal spot, having gained both handicap wins off 2lb lower in Class 5; others preferred at this level.
runners.comment: Not in the ideal spot, having gained both handicap wins off 2lb lower in Class 5

runners.name: Tyger Bay
runners.spotlight: Retains plenty of ability aged nine and rallied well for neck success over C&D most recently, taking AW record to 11-69; back up the weights but won off this mark in 2022 and 2024; may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Rallied well over C&D last time, taking AW record to 11-69; may remain competitive

runners.name: Gold Star Hero
runners.spotlight: Less exposed on AW than on turf; form in this sphere comprises a success in C&D novice in 2024 and creditable second in Lingfield handicap (5f) on penultimate appearance; gelded since latest; one to consider.
runners.comment: Form on AW comprises a C&D success and creditable second at Lingfield; one to consider

runners.name: Hierarchy
runners.spotlight: Normally a fairly consistent sort but that's not been the case in 2026, with Lingfield success (penultimate start) his standout performance; failed to beat a rival latest; hard to support with confidence.
runners.comment: Lingfield win (penultimate start) is his standout performance this year

races.name: Exclusive Club Dining Experience Handicap

runners.name: Faithful Dream
runners.spotlight: Maiden whose last two races (6f) haven't panned out ideally, unlucky second at Kempton on penultimate; may gain compensation provided better luck materialises.
runners.comment: Maiden whose last two races haven't panned out ideally; unlucky second on penultimate

runners.name: Saeculamation
runners.spotlight: Opened his account in similar event over C&D in December; has regressed in two starts elsewhere since but this return to Chelmsford may prompt a turnaround.
runners.comment: Returns to the scene of his December win; turnaround is possible

runners.name: A Lott Of Kane
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to finishing behind two of today's rivals at Wolverhampton last week; encouraging efforts otherwise in handicaps but needs to rebound.
runners.comment: Behind two of today's rivals at Wolverhampton last week; needs to rebound

runners.name: Magnificent Mel
runners.spotlight: Ran well at Chelmsford in December and Wolverhampton on penultimate start but isn't guaranteed to repeat the form, being an inconsistent maiden.
runners.comment: Inconsistent maiden who isn't sure to show best form

runners.name: In Denial
runners.spotlight: Out of a five-time winner for her connections; promising fourth at Wolverhampton (6f; handicap debut) last week and looks likely to take another step forward; respected.
runners.comment: Promising fourth at Wolverhampton last week on handicap debut; respected

runners.name: Amakhala
runners.spotlight: Has finished last in most of his five runs; fitting of headgear needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Fitting of headgear needs to make a big difference

runners.name: What A Tahoo
runners.spotlight: Best effort when fourth at Newcastle on handicap debut and most recent 6f attempt; could go well, returned to this distance.
runners.comment: Best effort when fourth at Newcastle in most recent 6f attempt
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Hurricane Ann
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden who ran creditably, fifth of nine, in Kempton handicap (dropped to 6f) most recently; possibilities provided she builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Ran creditably at Kempton on handicap debut last time; possibilities

runners.name: Go Lockers Go
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite, having taken a good hold, over 5f at Chelmsford (AW/handicap debut) when last seen; may settle better and show progress with hood refitted.
runners.comment: May settle better and show progress with hood refitted on reappearance
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing D Turner B Roberts

races.name: Fairwood Brasserie Handicap

runners.name: Kings Cruiser
runners.spotlight: Has two pieces of encouraging form over 2m since handicapping, while shaping as if this return to shorter will suit; first win looks possible.
runners.comment: Has encouraging form since handicapping; drop back in trip looks a plus; possibilities

runners.name: Dynamite Defense
runners.spotlight: Gained his Irish win on AW; posted an encouraging effort, keeping-on third, at Lingfield (1m4f) returned to this sphere on seasonal debut; interesting.
runners.comment: Gained his Irish win on AW; ran encouragingly returned to this sphere on reappearance

runners.name: Take The Boat
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 3412 (over 9.5f-1m4f) in Class 6 this year; likely to be thereabouts back at this level, provided she stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 3412 in Class 6 this year; likely to be thereabouts

runners.name: Fiddlers Green
runners.spotlight: All wins at 1m3f-1m4f; hasn't fully convinced with stamina in his 1m5f/1m6f attempts, including latest start; may again get outstayed.
runners.comment: Hasn't fully convinced with stamina in 1m5f/1m6f attempts
runners.owner: Phil Hart And The Cross Racing Club

runners.name: Angelardo
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo; opened his account in Newcastle event upped to 2m last time; perhaps not crying out for this return to shorter but is unexposed.
runners.comment: Off the mark in Newcastle event upped to 2m last time; unexposed

runners.name: El Ghaawy
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; ran well (close fourth) over 1m4f at Wolverhampton most recently; in the mix, provided he backs up that effort.
runners.comment: Maiden; ran well at Wolverhampton last time; in the mix
runners.owner: Jwi Building Solutions J Kelly & Partner

runners.name: Bohemian Breeze
runners.spotlight: On last winning mark but his two efforts this year leave a big doubt over current form; best watched unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: On last winning mark but has a doubt over current form

runners.name: Smokey Malone
runners.spotlight: Prevailed narrowly at Chelmsford (1m6f) last time to complete a hat-trick; climbing back up the weights but warrants respect in current form.
runners.comment: Prevailed narrowly at Chelmsford last time to complete a hat-trick; respected

races.name: Racing Welfare Handicap

runners.name: Clover Time
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden but six times a runner-up, including over C&D; his form dipped at Southwell eight weeks ago but it will be no surprise to see him bounce back quickly.
runners.comment: 0-12 but he's finished 2nd on six occasions; can quickly bounce back from latest quiet run

runners.name: Initial Blue
runners.spotlight: Made all for an easy win at Kempton (6f) last month when blinkers were added for the first time; two solid efforts (again over 6f) behind a thriving rival have followed; creeping up the weights and dropping in trip but still more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Made all at Kempton last month; followed it with 2 good runs at Wolverhampton; down to 5f

runners.name: Cabeza De Llave
runners.spotlight: Three of his six wins have come over C&D but the latest was back in October 2024; ended 2025 with a string of placed efforts and last month's third, after 135 days off, was encouraging; needs more up in class but likely to give his running.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit and ran creditably after a break last month; up in class today though

runners.name: Manhattan Chute
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 50-1 surprise when winning a 5f handicap at Lingfield in January; he hasn't beaten a rival in three starts since, however, and now arrives with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Sprung a 50-1 surprise at Lingfield in January but he's finished last in three runs since
runners.owner: J May R L Field W Trenchard J Pook

runners.name: Diamond Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Won this race 12 months ago off a 1lb higher mark; finished a close second at Lingfield three weeks ago and he has plenty to recommend him.
runners.comment: Won this race last year off 1lb higher; good second latest; plenty to recommend him

runners.name: Fiorella Princess
runners.spotlight: Her record at Chelmsford reads 1223; her latest third was a well-beaten one, however, and more minor money may be her best hope.
runners.comment: Good course record but she was a well-beaten third last time and she'll need more tonight

runners.name: Skellig Isle
runners.spotlight: Of major interest on her 2yo form; generally struggled last year but she starts out for her new stable on a dangerous mark; betting should be revealing.
runners.comment: Struggled last year but dropped a long way in the weights; betting useful on stable debut

runners.name: Bang On The Bell
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner down the years but in a slump at present; he has dropped 19lb in five runs since November however and returning to 5f with headgear back on could spark a revival.
runners.comment: Dropped 19lb in five runs this winter; risky but likely to turn things round at some point

races.name: For The Love Of Horses Handicap

runners.name: King David
runners.spotlight: Below par in visor last time; posted consistent efforts (424) in blinkers earlier in February and could go well with the blinds reapplied; successful only once (in 2024) but current mark is workable.
runners.comment: Could go well in refitted blinkers; successful only once but current mark is workable

runners.name: My Boy Harry
runners.spotlight: Has a broadly solid record (12135) since wearing blinkers and the January wins were his last two visits to Chelmsford; needs to prove he can defy current mark but the return to this venue may pay dividends.
runners.comment: Good record since wearing blinkers features wins in last two visits to Chelmsford

runners.name: Sanditon
runners.spotlight: Opened his account with a success off current mark, getting up close home to beat King David by a neck, over C&D in February; enters calculations on that form but has failed to back it up since.
runners.comment: Opened his account in C&D event in February; has failed to back up that form since

runners.name: Bint Havana Gold
runners.spotlight: Has inconsistent recent form figures but was hindered by meeting trouble last time when bidding for a Wolverhampton double (6f); scored twice off current mark at Chelmsford (7f/6f) in 2025; fighting chance.
runners.comment: Met trouble last time; scored twice off current mark at Chelmsford in 2025
runners.owner: Alan & Christine Bright And John Henwood

runners.name: Shades Of May
runners.spotlight: Looks one for in-play punters as the only time he has dominated from the outset resulted in sole success, which was gained at Chelmsford (1m; set fast pace) on penultimate start; unexposed under such tactics.
runners.comment: One for in-play punters as chance may depend on whether he leads from the outset

runners.name: Francisco
runners.spotlight: Not totally straightforward but shaped nicely, running-on fourth in a race that panned out unfavourably, at Lingfield (7f) on seasonal debut; gained sole win off 3lb higher under Alfie Redman last April; one to consider.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely on reappearance; gained sole win off 3lb higher last April; in the mix

runners.name: Macarone
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over sprint trips; ran respectably at Wolverhampton most recently; not crying out for the return to 7f and Polytrack is an unknown, with all of his AW form on Tapeta; wears new headgear.
runners.comment: Not crying out for return to 7f and Polytrack is an unknown

runners.name: The Organiser
runners.spotlight: Scored off 3lb higher on turf in September; however, far from consistent otherwise for current stable, latterly at Lingfield since returned to AW; well weighted but isn't a solid option.
runners.comment: Won on turf in September; far from consistent otherwise for current yard

runners.name: Orbital Chime
runners.spotlight: Won off 3lb higher in November when with Katie Scott; not in the same form since but attracted support at Wolverhampton last week when making debut for new yard and catching the attention of the stewards; possibilities if taking well to new headgear.
runners.comment: Caught the attention of the stewards last week on stable debut; possibilities

runners.name: Devious Devan
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; placed a few times in 2024 but wasn't in anything like the same form last year; chance largely depends on how well he responds to a physical tweak (wind surgery since last run).
runners.comment: Maiden; chance depends on how well he responds to a physical tweak (wind surgery)

runners.name: Caragio
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts last year for Martin Dunne but was previously a dual winner for Roger Teal, including under Adam Kirby who has recently commenced his training career and now houses this gelding; interesting off reduced mark dropped in class, with a revival possible.
runners.comment: Interesting off a reduced mark dropped in class, with revival possible on stable debut

races.name: Injured Jockeys Fund Handicap

runners.name: Aneirins Sword
runners.spotlight: Two wins and four seconds, the latest when chasing home Zipwire 16 days ago, since moving into handicaps this year; he will need to step forward for the extra distance but he seems likely to be in the picture.
runners.comment: Two wins and four seconds from six handicap runs this year; up in trip today; solid

runners.name: Berkshire Schmokin
runners.spotlight: Improved for the step up to 9.4f when winning at Wolverhampton last month, his second handicap; only fourth behind two of tonight's rivals at Wolverhampton 16 days ago; needs to raise his game but that is possible.
runners.comment: Behind two of today's rivals at Wolverhampton 16 days ago; still has time to do better
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Zipwire
runners.spotlight: Had looked exposed before winning at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in late December; followed up when narrowly denying Anerin's Sword back at Wolverhampton (9.4f) 16 days ago, making it 2-2 in cheekpieces; should give it a good go in his hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: Improved for headgear, winning his last two starts at Wolverhampton; respected
runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore

runners.name: Port Darwin
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to win a 1m nursery at Musselburgh last August; he hasn't built on it in three runs since but stepping up in trip could unlock some progress.
runners.comment: 1m nursery win as a 2yo; not progressed since that success; new trip could help

runners.name: Pantiles Gift
runners.spotlight: Promise in his first three starts, recording a peak RPR of 67 when second at Bath in October (1m, good to soft); disappointing on slower ground three weeks later (absent since) but he steps up in trip for his handicap debut and still has some potential.
runners.comment: Below par on soft ground when last seen; up in trip for handicap debut; retains potential

runners.name: Engross
runners.spotlight: Three runs as a 2yo, showing his best form when a keeping-on fourth of 12 at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in December; gelded since; bred to appreciate the longer trip and he's an interesting contender on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Gelded since latest start; likely improver upped in trip for handicap debut
